Low employee engagement is at a record high, costing the global economy an estimated [$8.9 trillion or 9% of global GDP](https://www.gallup.com/workplace/349484/state-of-the-global-workplace-2022-report.aspx#ite-645944), according to a [2024 Gallup report](https://www.gallup.com/workplace/349484/state-of-the-global-workplace-2022-report.aspx#ite-645944). Cleaning and security employees working on distributed teams are particularly vulnerable to feeling less engaged, as they are isolated and dispersed locations making it difficult for them to stay connected.Globally, more than [20% of employees](https://www.gallup.com/workplace/349484/state-of-the-global-workplace.aspx) experience daily loneliness, which directly impacts their productivity, according to [research](https://www.gallup.com/workplace/349484/state-of-the-global-workplace-2022-report.aspx#ite-645944). ## Managing distributed teams

It can be easy to overlook the importance of engaging and connecting with distributed teams – from management to back-office staff, to cleaners and guards in the field – simply because of the variance in how often distributed teams interact face-to-face with employers. The [Society for Human Resource Management (SHRM)](https://www.shrm.org/labs/resources/building-a-connected-workforce-key-insights-on-employee-engagement) states that employee engagement is a pivotal force for shaping workplace outcomes. Additionally, emotional commitment and behavioral dedication drive the retention, productivity and satisfaction that companies seek. So, how can employers improve work environments when it comes to managing distributed teams, especially cleaners or guards, by keeping employees engaged while they work independently and offsite from a centralized office? The following is a list of pointers:

- Access technology that provides open communication channels
- Empower employees through technology-driven access
- Leverage technology as a tool to attract and retain a workforce
- Utilize dependable software that works in good times and uncertain times

A software solution with a [self-service portal](https://teamsoftware.com/blog/security-guard-scheduling-2/) can offer a hub of information that workers can independently access within the field. Administrators can regularly update those portals with view-only information pertaining to payroll details, tax information or benefit packages. Employees who want that type of information can access those details anytime.

## Improving company culture via communication

Social isolation and chronic loneliness are primary causes for some of the negative physical and mental health effects that workers experience, according to [researchers](https://www.gallup.com/workplace/349484/state-of-the-global-workplace-2022-report.aspx#ite-645944). However, boosting engagement encourages positive employee experiences by placing employees first. Industry-specific cleaning and security software can help support [distributed workforce engagement](https://teamsoftware.com/blog/distributed-workforce-management/). Instead of treating technology solutions as a lifeline, business owners can treat them as necessary improvements to operations and proactive precautions against crises. An investment in technology directly links frontline cleaners and guards to other areas of the business. Once implemented, technology can create open lines of communication by allowing workers to send and receive messages across platforms to keep workers engaged. In-person work interactions are not necessary to provide a benefit. [A Gallup study](https://www.gallup.com/workplace/336935/wellbeing-at-work.aspx) found that all forms of social time – particularly phone, video or texting – are associated with a better mood. However, technological interactions such as messaging have thresholds, which means that moods drop after moderate amounts.With the right solution, distributed teams should be able to use mobile phone technology to ask questions, reply to work-site instructions, manage safety and job-site checkpoints and have a direct line of communication with a manager in real time.

# A Behind-the-Scenes Look at TikTok Localization Strategy

![4fQCl7g84wsfePcSseYsZo_tiktok](https://alconost.com/static/pages-images/blog-ctfassets/4fQCl7g84wsfePcSseYsZo_tiktok.png)

[TikTok](https://www.tiktok.com/), a hot short video app by ByteDance, is well known as a cultural phenomenon. In this article we will dive into something that has received little attention: how the TikTok team balances its global presence in 150 countries with agile product development.

First off, let us give you a short intro and throw in some stats.

TikTok was launched internationally in 2017, and it quickly started gaining popularity. In the span of a few short years the app has seen explosive growth, from 800+ million downloads [in 2018](https://influencermarketinghub.com/tiktok-growth/) to a whopping [2.6 billion downloads](https://sensortower.com/blog/top-rated-apps-2020) by January 2021.

Here are three factors that have contributed to TikTok’s mind-blowing popularity:

- A smart marketing strategy involving influencers and exciting [‘hashtag challenges’](https://www.tiktok.com/forgood?lang=en)

- Keeping up with the trends AND being a trend-setter

- Localization strategy (TikTok is available in 75 languages across 150 countries).


Let’s talk about how the TikTok localization workflow is set up. As one of TikTok’s localization vendors, here at [Alconost](https://alconost.com/en/localization/apps) we have the inside story.

### The founder’s vision: ‘Going global is a must’

ByteDance founder Zhang Yiming has always had a global vision for his company.

“China is home to only one fifth of the world’s internet users. Going global is a must,” [Zhang Yiming told](https://kr-asia.com/a-closer-look-at-tik-toks-expansion-overseas) KrAsia in an interview.

These global ambitions were backed up by cutting-edge technology. One of the first ByteDance’s products, the popular news aggregator Jinri Toutiao, employs AI-based recommendation algorithms.The app was successfully expanded to overseas markets, and this experience along with the app’s technology were employed in other ByteDance products.

### Why localize TikTok into 75 languages?

Since the TikTok interface is easy to navigate, you might think localization wouldn’t be all that critical—especially not into 75 languages. But the TikTok team strives for a seamless user experience, and localization is an important part of this.

TikTok has been working with Alconost since early 2018. Besides common languages like German, Spanish, Russian and Arabic, our translation languages include Afrikaans, Tagalog, and others.

Why include less common languages? Our previous case studies [have shown](https://alconost.com/en/blog/wachanga) that successful mobile apps usually go with several dozen languages in order to cover as wide an audience as possible, and it pays off.

By 2019, TikTok and Alconost had streamlined the translation workflow, and multinational localization was in full swing.

In February 2019 TikTok soared from ranking below 100th place to join the top 20 most downloaded apps in the world (see the spike in growth on the chart), and has shown steady growth ever since.

![4nFuETZ9xqpGnpjrS5pi3d_tiktok2](https://alconost.com/static/pages-images/blog-ctfassets/4nFuETZ9xqpGnpjrS5pi3d_tiktok2.png)

Spike in TikTok’s growth on Google Play starting in February 2019 ( [source: Apptrace](https://www.apptrace.com/app/com.zhiliaoapp.musically))

Now let’s take a look at how the TikTok team manages continuous delivery and localization.

### Agile localization customized to TikTok’s needs

TikTok’s agile workflow with its frequent updates calls for [continuous localization](https://alconost.com/en/localization/continuous-localization). Over the past two years of collaboration, the TikTok localization team and Alconost have defined a process that ensures swift delivery while also meeting standards for quality.

New strings for translation arrive twice a week under tight deadlines. We work on [Crowdin](https://alconost.com/en/localization/crowdin-alconost), our favorite localization platform, which allows automatic uploading and exporting of strings.

Constant interface updates require sharing a lot of context, which is easy on Crowdin: clients can specify the context for a standalone string, add screenshots to it, and communicate with linguists in a chat. The TikTok localization team and Alconost’s project manager are constantly in touch, so as to swiftly resolve any questions and handle urgent translations when they arise.

As high-quality localization is very important to TikTok, all translations undergo [proofreading](https://alconost.com/en/localization/proofreading) before the team rolls out a new update. Linguists also perform monthly QA to ensure quality.

With a huge volume of text (150K+ words) and frequent updates, consistency is essential. Glossaries and translation memories are important tools for this, and we maintain both for this ongoing project.

> “As we roll out updates about once a week, we have very tight deadlines. With our high requirements for localization quality, the vendors have to be at top performance. Sometimes we even need a 24-hour turnaround time, and we have been pleased with Alconost’s ability to meet our deadlines and quality requirements.”
>
> \- Dong Ming, head of localization at TikTok

Creating videos that speak to global audiences used to mean choosing between expensive dubbing services or settling for subtitles. What if you could translate and dub your content in under an hour while maintaining your authentic voice and personality across languages?

The multilingual video landscape has transformed dramatically. Content creators now face unprecedented demand for localized content, with dubbed videos consistently outperforming subtitled versions in engagement metrics. The traditional approach—hiring translators, voice actors, and video editors—can cost hundreds of dollars and take weeks per video. This bottleneck prevents creators from capitalizing on global opportunities and limits business expansion into new markets.

Enter [Perso AI](https://perso.ai/de/): a comprehensive platform that streamlines the video localization workflow from upload to publish in minutes rather than weeks. This guide reveals the exact process used by creators to reach international audiences efficiently.

## Why Speed and Quality Matter in Multilingual Video Creation

The data tells a compelling story: multilingual content drives measurable business growth. Videos dubbed in local languages generate significantly higher engagement rates than subtitled versions, particularly on mobile devices where reading subtitles can be challenging.

For businesses, the benefits extend beyond viewer metrics. Companies using multilingual video content report faster market penetration, improved brand recognition in new regions, and enhanced customer satisfaction. Internal use cases are equally impactful—multilingual training videos increase comprehension rates and reduce onboarding time for diverse teams.

The challenge lies in execution speed. In competitive markets, being early to localize content often determines success. [A product launch video](https://perso.ai/use-case/onboarding-video) that takes weeks to translate may miss its peak opportunity. [Educational content,](https://perso.ai/use-case/online-courses) on the other hand, benefits greatly from reaching learners in their native language shortly after release.

Traditional localization workflows introduce unavoidable delays. Voice actor coordination, studio bookings, post-production editing, and quality checks often extend timelines by several weeks per language. For creators working across multiple languages, these delays quickly add up.

## What Makes Perso AI a Strong Choice for Fast Video Localization

Perso AI addresses these workflow challenges through integrated AI technologies working together in a single platform. Rather than focusing only on basic translation, Perso AI combines [voice cloning](https://perso.ai/de/voice-cloning), cultural intelligence, and [precision lip-sync](https://perso.ai/de/ai-lip-sync) into a unified workflow.

### Voice Cloning That Preserves Authenticity

Instead of replacing your voice with a generic narrator, Perso AI analyzes speech patterns, tone, and delivery to generate multilingual versions that closely match the original speaker. # How to Grow your Channel with the YouTube Multi Audio Track Feature

![Dylan de Koning](https://www.voquent.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/98861664960678-5.png)

[Dylan de Koning](https://www.voquent.com/blog/author/dylan/)

31 July 2023

![How to Grow your Channel with the YouTube Multi Audio Track Feature - Voquent](https://www.voquent.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/YT.jpeg)

### Expand your global reach with YouTube dubbing in any language

In an effort to boost inclusivity for its audience and global growth for its content creators, YouTube has unveiled its new multi-language audio track feature, which is set to revolutionise localisation on the platform.

For audiences, this allows us to discover new creators who wouldn’t usually post content in a language we speak, and in turn, provides a huge opportunity for creators to grow their global following.

## What is the YouTube Multi-Audio Track Feature?

This new localisation feature allows content creators to **break through language barriers and engage global audiences** like never before.

YouTube

Tap to unmute

[Watch video on YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vY31qIX7LzQ&source_ve_path=MTc4NDI0)

Error 153

Video player configuration error

[Visit YouTube to search for more videos](https://www.youtube.com/)

Audiences can now toggle the language options of a video to watch it in their native tongue, should content creators provide them with that option via dubbed versions of their YouTube videos.

If you’re new to the platform, you can [learn how to make a YouTube video](https://www.adobe.com/express/create/video/youtube) here.

## One Channel

While YouTube dubbing isn’t new, the multi-language audio track feature has streamlined the process for easier use.

Previously, YouTubers would create multiple versions of their channel for different languages, but now various language options can be presented on each individual video, all from one main channel. This minimises the amount of work necessary for content creators to reach new international audiences, with an easier process for the implementation of dubbed YouTube content.

## Monetisation & Mr Beast

While this new multi-language feature is a huge boost to accessibility on YouTube, it will also make more money for both the platform and creators alike.

Advertisers will be pleased to learn that audiences having the option to hear content in their own language rather than using subtitles leads to **higher engagement rates and longer watch times.** The more time someone watches a video, the greater the ad exposure. This allows advertisers more opportunities to appear on content aimed at their target market, and this also generates more profit for the platform.

Similarly, more profit for YouTube via advertisement creates **more profit for content creators** too. Multiple language options significantly increase the number of people who can watch your content internationally and increases the level of engagement and enjoyment for this new audience. It also makes it more likely for international audiences to subscribe; knowing your channel now facilitates their language preferences.

## Quick Summary

Video has become the default language of B2B marketing. Buyers expect it at every touchpoint, from their first Google search to their final vendor evaluation. But until recently, professional video production meant choosing between substantial budgets or amateur-looking content that undermined your credibility.

AI-powered video tools have dismantled those barriers. The capabilities available for free today would have cost your company thousands just three years ago. AI avatars that speak 140+ languages. Text-to-video generation in minutes. Professional templates that require zero design experience.

Startups can now produce content that rivals what enterprise teams ship.

Here’s our roundup of the **best free AI video tools,** categorized by what type of marketing video you’re producing.

## Explainer Videos

[**Synthesia**](https://www.synthesia.io/) leads the free explainer space with three minutes of monthly content featuring AI avatars that speak in over 140 languages. # How to Communicate Shift Changes Effectively in Multilingual Teams

![multilingual shift communication](https://myshyft.blob.core.windows.net/media/uploads/2025/03/multilingual-shift-communication.jpg)

Managing employee schedules in diverse, multilingual teams presents unique communication challenges. When team members speak different languages, communicating shift changes effectively becomes critical to maintaining operational efficiency and preventing costly miscommunications. In today’s global workplace, organizations must develop robust strategies to ensure information about schedule adjustments reaches all employees clearly, regardless of language barriers.

This comprehensive guide explores best practices for communicating shift changes in multilingual environments. We’ll examine technological solutions, cultural considerations, and practical frameworks that help organizations overcome language barriers while ensuring schedule changes are communicated efficiently across diverse teams. Whether you’re managing a multinational retail operation, a diverse hospitality staff, or a multilingual manufacturing team, these strategies will help you create a more inclusive and effective shift communication system.

## Understanding the Multilingual Scheduling Challenge

Schedule changes are inevitable in any workplace, but they present unique challenges when your workforce speaks multiple languages. Poor communication across language barriers can lead to missed shifts, unnecessary overtime, and decreased employee satisfaction. Understanding these challenges is the first step toward creating an effective system.

- **Misinterpreted Instructions**: Even when translated, subtle nuances in scheduling instructions can be lost, leading to confusion about start times or responsibilities.
- **Inconsistent Communication Channels**: Different team members may prefer varied communication methods based on cultural or language preferences.
- **Feedback Barriers**: Employees may hesitate to ask clarifying questions when they don’t fully understand schedule changes due to language insecurity.
- **Time-Sensitive Updates**: Last-minute shift changes become particularly problematic when translation delays impact timely delivery of information.
- **Cultural Differences**: Varied cultural norms regarding time, authority, and work obligations can affect how scheduling information is received and acted upon.

Modern [employee scheduling](https://www.myshyft.com/employee-scheduling/) solutions have evolved to address these challenges, incorporating features specifically designed for multilingual workforces. By implementing the right tools and protocols, organizations can transform potential communication barriers into opportunities for greater efficiency and inclusion.

[![Shyft CTA](https://www.myshyft.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Shyft-CTA-3.png)](https://web.myshyft.com/sign-up)

## Creating a Multilingual Communication Framework

Establishing a systematic approach to multilingual communication forms the foundation of effective shift change management. A well-designed framework ensures that all employees receive critical scheduling information in a format they can understand, regardless of their primary language.

- **Language Assessment**: Conduct regular surveys to identify all languages spoken within your organization and the proficiency levels of employees.
- **Documentation Standards**: Create standardized templates for schedule changes that can be easily translated while maintaining visual consistency.
- **Translation Resources**: Establish relationships with professional translators or implement technology solutions for routine scheduling communications.
- **Communication Hierarchy**: Define clear processes for cascading important scheduling information through various languages and departments.
- **Accessible Policy Documents**: Ensure that scheduling policies and procedures are available in all languages represented in your workforce.

According to [research on multilingual team communication](https://www.myshyft.com/blog/multilingual-team-communication/), organizations that implement structured communication frameworks see up to 35% fewer schedule-related errors. The investment in building these systems pays dividends through improved operational efficiency and higher employee satisfaction rates.

## **What is Video Translation?**

Video translation involves converting the spoken or written content of a video into another language. The process can include translating subtitles, adding voiceovers, or utilizing AI-powered tools to generate translations automatically.

Why you may ask? Well, as discussed earlier, translating your videos allows you to share content with non-native speakers, breaking down language barriers and expanding your audience.

‍

## **Importance of Video Translation**

Translating videos goes beyond a simple word-for-word conversion. Conveying the original message, tone, and context to a different audience is essential. Let us look at some key reasons why we believe video translation is important:

- Making your content accessible to people who speak different languages significantly expands your audience and your global reach.
- Presenting content in viewers native language increases engagement and improves overall connection.
- Connecting with audiences on a cultural level enhances the impact and effectiveness of your message, fostering a deeper cultural connection.
- Offering multilingual content helps you stand out in a crowded market and provides a competitive advantage.

## **Top Video Translation Tools**

## **Step-by-Step Guide on How to Translate a Video**

Translating a video may seem daunting, but following a structured approach can simplify the process. Here's how you can translate your video effectively:

### **Step 1: Prepare Your Video Content**

Ensure your video is finalized and ready for translation. Export it in a suitable format and gather any available scripts or transcripts.

### **Step 2: Choose the Right Translation Tool**

Select a video translation tool that fits your needs. Consider factors like language support, ease of use, and integration with your workflow. For advanced features and extensive language support, [CAMB.AI](https://www.camb.ai/) is an excellent choice.

### **Step 3: Transcribe the Original Audio**

Create a transcript of the original audio. Accurate transcription is crucial for precise translation. Some tools offer automatic transcription, but reviewing and editing for accuracy is recommended.

### **Step 4: Translate the Transcript**

Translate the transcript into your target language. This can be done manually by a professional translator or by using AI-powered translation tools. Paying attention to idioms, cultural references, and context ensures they are appropriately adapted.

### **Step 5: Add Subtitles or Dubbing**

Decide whether to add translated subtitles or dub the audio. Subtitles involve overlaying the translated text on the video, while dubbing replaces the original audio with a voiceover in the target language.

### **Step 6: Sync the Translation with the Video**

Align the translated text or audio with the video timeline. Precise synchronization ensures that the translation matches the visuals and original speech patterns.

### **Step 7: Review and Edit**

Watch the translated video to check for errors, timing issues, or awkward phrasing. Editing at this stage helps to refine the final product.

### **Step 8: Export and Share**

Export the translated video in your desired format and share it with your target audience. Promoting through the right channels will maximize reach.

## **Common Challenges in Video Translation**

Translating videos presents certain challenges. Being aware of these can help in planning and executing the translation more effectively. For example:

- Translating idioms, humor, or cultural references may require adaptation rather than direct translation to handle cultural nuances.
- Syncing subtitles or dubbed audio can be technically complex, requiring specialized software to overcome technical limitations.
- Ensuring accuracy in translation and synchronization demands meticulous review and possibly professional assistance to maintain quality control.
- Managing translations for multiple languages increases complexity in coordination and consistency when dealing with multiple languages.

‍

## **Tips for Effective Video Translation**

- Understand your audience by tailoring the translation to their cultural context and preferences.
- Invest in quality translation, whether by hiring professional translators or using high-quality AI models.

## What is sales enablement?

Sales enablement is a strategic initiative that aims to equip sales teams with the right processes, content and tools to meet business goals.

Strengthening the partnership between marketing and sales teams empowers businesses to attract, engage and win over prospects more effectively. You will know your sales enablement strategy is working if revenue is increasing, average deal size is higher, and sales cycles are shorter.

## Key challenges in sales enablement

![](https://www.celum.com/en/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2021/02/SalesEnablement_3.png)

**Siloed leadership and team structure** lead to misunderstandings and lack of a holistic strategy. For example, marketing may be using gated content on social media to attract upper funnel leads, which sales teams haven’t been trained to nurture appropriately. This often results in the sales rep offering a demo too soon and scaring off qualified prospects.

**Separate folders and files** make it hard for sales and marketing teams to create, share and distribute content. This often leads to sales “doing their thing” and using outdated marketing material or creating their own without the marketing team’s knowledge.

**Lack of high-quality, relevant content.** 90% of B2B sellers don’t use sales material because it is irrelevant, outdated and difficult to customize, according to [Forrester](https://www.forrester.com/report/Make+Sales+Conversations+An+Integral+Part+Of+Your+Content+Marketing+Plans/-/E-RES128016).

## How to create an effective sales enablement strategy

​As with any strategic initiative, sales enablement strategy starts with setting clear objectives and measurable goals.

Getting two separate organizations to work together is complex, so planning and alignment from the very beginning are crucial. Senior leaders in the marketing and sales organization have a particularly important role to play and should lead by example to drive inclusion and collaboration.

## How marketing leaders are winning at sales enablement

We spoke to senior marketers to understand how they are successfully driving sales enablement in their organizations.

The future of sales and marketing requires the seamless integration of ‘art’ (creative, brand, culture) and ‘science’ (data & media). Sales and marketing teams must come together as one unit to make a difference in their industry. It’s the best way to understand the buyer persona and target ideal customers, which will result in better lead conversion and revenue generation.

![Arslan Ashraf](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%20210%20140%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)

Have your marketing managers join sales calls to understand customer wishes and pain-points. Invite sales managers to participate in creative brainstorms. Then, turn those insights into powerful content and strategies that add value in the most crucial parts of the funnel for your organization. Opening doors to both perspectives creates an environment of continuous learning and cooperation.

![Paula Monteiro](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%20210%20140%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)

To really strengthen a partnership between two teams, marketing shouldn’t simply stop at getting people to the website. Supply your sales team with extra materials so they can have a better conversation with potential clients. What works best are one-page flyers that condense case studies, information on your technology and the benefits.

# Guide to E-Learning Localization and E-Learning Translation Requirements

Hanna Lysenko30 Sep 2024

![E-Learning Localization and E-Learning Translation Requirements](https://thewordpoint.com/uploads/17/elearning-localization-guide.jpg)

While the scope and outlook for e-learning has always been excellent, it especially experienced a surge in popularity in the last 5 years. You would be surprised to know that experts analyze that the industry will surpass the $320 billion mark by next year. As a corporate entity, an individual, or an academic institution, you may already be thinking about jumping on the bandwagon and optimizing your e-learning course or corporate training manuals to reach a wider target audience. That's an excellent idea that may propel you two steps ahead of your competitor. However, first, it would do you a world of good if you understand how you will do that. Below, we will take a deep dive into understanding the core e-learning translation requirements and discuss the importance of [e-learning content localization](https://thewordpoint.com/services/translation-service/elearning-content-translation).

## Understanding E-Learning, It's Wide Occurrence, and Why You Should Consider Investing in It

Let's briefly consider the meaning of the phrase e-learning. The term is short for “electronic learning”. This type of learning is used in corporate, academic, and individual education or training programs via digital means, delivering the content to a wider group of employees or students throughout the globe. E-learning can come in a variety of formats, such as one-on-one tutoring/training, business seminars, educational webinars, digital classrooms, online corporate training manuals, educational apps, and much more. By having your content accurately translated along with investing in e-learning localization, you won't just be able to reach students, clients, or employees from all over the world but also to deliver your target audience engaging, exciting, easy-to-understand, and relatable learning educational content.

E-learning is a powerful education and training tool that is used by individuals and companies to save costs related to in-person instruction and simultaneously help their learners and employees with their skills and professional development. This format also helps students learn in a more comfortable environment and at their own pace.

However, while there can be no compromise in the quality of your content translation – it is also important to understand that there is another important element to make your e-learning content that much more captivating and engaging – localization (more on this later).

## The Merits of E-Learning Translation and the Problem It Solves

Let's quickly glance over the immediate advantages of [e-learning translation](https://thewordpoint.com/services/translation-service/elearning) and why it is fast becoming one of the best ways to reach and captivate a more global audience.

### It Will Attract a Much Wider Target Audience

One of the most wonderful benefits of digital learning is that an individual, institution, or business entity can teach or train from anywhere in the world. E-learning doesn't require a lecture hall, classroom, or corporate training room. A professional individual or company will never be limited by any time zones – they can reach global users in varying time zones and multiple locations all at once – thanks to content translation and localization. This guarantees scalability, is more time-efficient, and can yield better and more positive results.

### Cost Mitigation

Another serious advantage of creating accurate and engaging e-learning materials is that it will help you save money. First and foremost, there are no distribution expenses. You will also avoid having to tediously store educational or training materials in physical locations. With digital learning, everything is securely stored on the cloud, ready to be accessed by authorized users anywhere and anytime.

### Help in Propelling Your Learning Outcomes

If you implement and abide by the core principles of e-learning translation requirements, you stand a higher chance of helping foster a community of engaging, better-trained, and educated individuals. According to reports, it has been identified that up to 60% of students were able to demonstrate better information retention when they were learning digitally. So yes, it can be worthwhile for individual trainers or educators and institutions and businesses to invest in e-learning translations.

## Important Factors That You Need to Implement for Producing Accurate and Superior E-Learning Translations and Localization

### Knowing Your Users or Audience

When it comes to e-learning translation requirements, this is by far one of the most important ones. While quality translations require accurate conversion of content into a given language, to make it so that your users truly resonate with learning material, you need to localize the product. Let's see how you can achieve that.

### Understanding Culture and Recognizing It Entirely

Before you translate your e-learning materials, it is vital that you first learn more about the culture of the target region. This involves acquiring an intimate knowledge of the social behaviors of the users in that region, their style and pattern of education, and how they essentially communicate. A good example can be learning about different types of cultural or religious holidays observed in that region. This is going to help you design an e-learning course that provides the users the opportunity to flexibly manage their studies or training.

### The Primary Language Spoken

Depending on the region you are targeting, there can be many local languages and dialects spoken in the country. However, you have to look for the most commonly spoken dialect and translate and localize your e-learning materials in that language. In addition, you will also need to identify the best formats and suitable styles of learning that your target audience can resonate with and engage with more.

### The Technical Infrastructure of the Region

Apart from providing quality translations and localizations for your e-learning content, you also need to ensure it derives value for your users. By evaluating the technological environment of the region and of the common user, you should be able to design an e-learning course that can be accessible by all in terms of online connectivity, software, and hardware requirements. This is especially true for people living in developing or poor countries.

### Prepare Your E-Learning Materials for Accurate Localization

To produce quality translations, you must prepare your e-learning content for easier localization. One of the first steps to achieving that is to use concise and clear language. The easier and more understandable the translation, the easier its localization. However, do not use any type of cultural references in the form of idioms or colloquialisms. They can be difficult to translate accurately and you may be lost in translation. The objective is to provide clear and simple translations to derive engagement and positive learning.

### Implement Images and Graphics

One of the most powerful elements in learning is accurately designed infographics, videos, and multimedia content (animations, etc). Be sure to include graphical imagery of any numbers, important explanations, statistics, etc., to make your content more absorbable, relevant, and engaging. ## What is e-learning?

To overview this topic comprehensively, let's designate e-learning and what is included in this term.

E-learning contains various materials and systems for online education. It could be SAAS platforms, online courses, LMS(learning management system,) online exam platforms, online training platforms for business, mobile educational applications, virtual classrooms, etc.

## What are the benefits of localizing e-learning products?

As well as the most significant part of industries, localization in e-learning is targeted at a few global things:

- **Expansion into new markets.** Localization is the tool that allows reach and access to new markets. Covering user groups' different educational needs in various regions enables you to infiltrate a specific area, get more audience, and generate additional revenue.
- **Increase retention and user experience quality.** Quality localization is necessary to be the best in the market and successfully compete with others. Precisely this process is responsible for content adaptation - appropriate texts, case studies, and symbols used. Localized materials that fit local educational standards and practices make the content more relevant, contextualized, and culturally appropriate, enhancing learners' happiness with the educational process.
- **Competitive advantage**: Investing in e-learning localization can provide a competitive edge in the global market. You raise your reputation as a worldwide provider.

## The importance of accurate and high-quality localization for e-learning

You can admit not-precise localization in some materials, like blog posts (it's not desirable but not so critical.) Such an approach will not work with e-learning; you should provide the exact information and translate terms with maximum accuracy. As localization is not only about translation, it's essential to take care of content adaptation. Just because of the different contexts, the cultural references from one region could be un-understandable in another. It concerns texts and visual materials, as well. Precise and accurate translations are crucial for improving customer service, product quality, and effective learning. Investing in professional translators for e-learning localization is important, as cost-saving in this area can lead to negative consequences.

## E-learning localization process

Let's overview the localization of e-learning in the example of an educational application - SAAS or mobile, because this type of software includes a lot of different content, so we can comprehensively overview the process. They contain not only courses and materials but reports, setting information, payment data, security data, and documentation for developers, managers, lectors, and users. Also, such systems often provide gamification mechanisms and grade books.

All the buttons, navigation, main icons, menu, etc.

Nick Laspina

[![](https://static.xx.fbcdn.net/rsrc.php/y9/r/1wu2QLdHhT9.webp)Share4](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?kid_directed_site=0&sdk=joey&u=https%3A%2F%2Fblog.vmgstudios.com%2Flocalization-versus-translation-video-marketing&display=popup&ref=plugin&src=share_button)

If you’re anything like me, you probably have quite the travel bug, but due to the many responsibilities of life, I’m not able to jump on a plane whenever I want and getaway. So, I resort to daydreaming to transport myself to my desired destination.

Instead of physically traveling, let’s instead go back in time to the United States in the mid-2000s where hoop earrings, Ed Hardy jeans, and No Fear t-shirts were everywhere. Where Gwen Stefani was trendsetting for an entire generation with her Harajuku-inspired fashion statements and where we’ll have no shortage in finding a wide array of Kanji (Japanese writing using Chinese characters) tattoos with incorrect meanings and usage.

![Ariana Grande’s “7 rings” kanji tattoo which actually reads “little charcoal grill.”](https://blog.vmgstudios.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Ariana-Grande-VMG-Studios.jpg?width=550&name=Ariana-Grande-VMG-Studios.jpg)

_Ariana Grande’s “7 rings” kanji tattoo actually reads “little charcoal grill.”_

( _I also want to make it known, as someone who has been studying Japanese for a while now, I totally understand Ariana’s mistake, and from a quick search, it seems like she’s also learning the language. Honest mistake and huge props to her for recognizing and embracing it.)_

Having a tattoo with an incorrect meaning isn’t ideal, and the same goes for your marketing materials. While your product video might not be as permanent as a tattoo, a big mistake like improper translation or localization may alienate part of your target audience and yield negative results.

[VMG producers](https://blog.vmgstudios.com/what-is-a-producer) have more than a decade of experience working with localizing everything from commercials to banner ads to video games and everything in between. We also have localization partners with extremely skilled and talented translators and localization experts from around the world. Pair those partnerships with VMG’s experience and you can rest easy knowing your creative content is in good hands.

While you’re getting your tattoo that has now gone through translation and cultural review, we’ll walk you through exactly what the difference is between translation and localization, why it’s important, _and_ how to use them in tandem to help reach new markets and show your tattoo to the entire world _(is the metaphor working, yet?)._

## **Localization vs. Translation: What’s the Difference?**

Basically, translation is the process of translating words or text from one language to another while localization is the process of making something local in character.

But we’re not here for the basics.

We’re all burgeoning polyglots and need to get into the real nitty-gritty.

So, let’s roll up our sleeves and hit this explanation with some elbow grease and tackle translation first and foremost.

## **What is Translation?**

Translation is taking something, let’s say a sign informing all employees to wash their hands after using the restroom, and putting it into another language. Cultural review and regionalisms aren’t at the forefront here since the message is simple.

![Bilingual sign for employees to wash their hands](https://blog.vmgstudios.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Bilingual-Sign-VMG-Studios.jpg?width=350&name=Bilingual-Sign-VMG-Studios.jpg)

My mom always told me to lead by example, so let’s explore a translation template with the English sentence, “The child played with the red ball.”

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Original Text** | **Target Language** | **Literal Translation** |
| The child played with the red ball. | Der Kind spielte mit dem roten Ball. | The child played with the red ball. |
| The child played with the red ball. | L’enfant a joué avec le ballon rouge. | The child played with the ball red. |
| The child played with the red ball. | 子供は赤いぼるで遊んだ | The child with red ball played. |

If you’re a native English speaker who doesn’t speak any other languages, you might still be able to pick out a few words in German and French through the process of elimination.

French is structured so that the adjective comes after the noun, whereas in German, the adjective(s) comes before the noun. This is also true of English and that makes sense since both English and German descended from the same root language: Proto-Germanic.

Let’s do a quick assessment. A few years ago, Rihanna got this French tattoo below. Taking what we learned above, can you spot the error?

![Rihanna's French tattoo which reads "rebelle fleur"](https://blog.vmgstudios.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Rihanna-Neck-Tattoo-VMG-Studios.jpg?width=500&name=Rihanna-Neck-Tattoo-VMG-Studios.jpg)

If you said, “Hey! Given what I just learned, that says, ‘Flower rebel’ not ‘Rebellious flower’” then you nailed our little assessment.

The rumor is (seeing how I don’t know Rihanna personally) she was intending to get a tattoo that read “Rebellious Flower” in French. Instead, she got a tattoo that reads “rebelle fleur” which translates to “Flower Rebel.”

Now, given that one of Rihanna’s largest demographic is English speakers in the U.S., many likely understand the message she was originally going for better than if it was properly organized in French. I’m not saying this is what Rihanna, or her tattoo artist Bang Bang (shout out to Bang Bang), had in mind when they organized it this way, but since it is a possibility, this begins to tip into the grey area between translation and localization.

In this case, it would be like localizing a French term into English while retaining the French text.

_In a quick summary, that is translation. You’re not changing much other than the words themselves._

Let’s take a look at that table again, specifically the column labeled “Literal Translation. This is extremely helpful in the translation process for those of us who don’t speak the target language.

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Original Text** | **Target Language** | **Literal Translation** |
| The child played with the red ball. | Der Kind spielte mit dem roten Ball. | The child played with the red ball. |
| The child played with the red ball. | L’enfant a joué avec le ballon rouge. | The child played with the ball red. |
| The child played with the red ball. | 子供は赤いボールで遊んだ | The child with red ball played. |

The literal translation gives you an idea of the syntax that makes up the sentence.

Now, we wouldn’t want to say, “The child plays with the ball red” in Engl

AI audio translation has become one of the most powerful technologies for global communication. In 2026, businesses, creators, educators, and remote teams rely on AI speech translation to instantly convert spoken language into another language with natural voice output.

From translating podcasts and YouTube videos to enabling multilingual meetings, modern tools now offer speech to speech translation that sounds remarkably human.

This guide explains everything you need to know about AI audio translation, including how it works, the technology behind it, and how to use AI voice translation online tools such as Fish Audio.

##### What Is AI Audio Translation?

AI audio translation is a technology that converts spoken language into another language automatically.
Instead of manually translating transcripts or hiring interpreters, AI systems process speech in three stages:

- Speech recognition

- Language translation

- Speech generation


This creates a speech to speech translation system where users can speak in one language and hear the output in another.
Today, many platforms offer AI voice translation online, allowing users to translate audio directly in a browser or through APIs.

### How AI Speech Translation Works

Modern AI speech translation relies on several advanced technologies working together.

##### 1\. Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR)

ASR converts spoken audio into text.

For example:

Audio input:

"Welcome to today's meeting."

ASR output:

Welcome to today's meeting.

Modern ASR models can understand accents, noisy environments, and conversational speech.

##### 2\. AI Language Translation

Once speech becomes text, the translation model processes it.

Large language models analyze:

- Grammar

- Context

- Meaning

- Cultural nuance


This ensures the translated sentence sounds natural instead of robotic.

##### 3\. Text-to-Speech (TTS)

Finally, Text-to-Speech converts the translated text into spoken audio.

Modern neural TTS systems generate:

- Natural voices

- Emotional tone

- Realistic pacing

- Human-like pronunciation


Platforms like [Fish Audio](https://fish.audio/) specialize in high-quality AI voice synthesis, allowing translated speech to sound smooth and professional. Fish Audio now has its very own audio translation tool known as [Fish Audio translate](https://fish.audio/translate-audio/).

### Types of AI Audio Translation

Not all translation tools work the same way. There are several common use cases for AI speech translation.

##### 1\. Real-Time Speech Translation

Real-time translation is used for live conversations.

Examples include:

- Video meetings

- Customer support calls

- Live presentations

- International conferences


The system must translate speech instantly with minimal delay.

##### 2\. Recorded Audio Translation

Recorded translation works with pre-existing audio files.

- Examples include:

- Podcasts

- Interviews

- Audiobooks

- Online courses

- YouTube videos


These systems often produce higher-quality results because they have more time to process the audio.

##### 3\. AI Voice Translation for Content Localization

Content creators often use AI voice translation online tools to localize content for global audiences.
Instead of recording multiple language versions manually, AI tools generate translated voices automatically.
Solutions like Fish Audio can recreate natural voices in multiple languages, making it easier to distribute content worldwide.

### Benefits of AI Audio Translation

AI translation technology offers several advantages for businesses and individuals.

##### Faster Communication

AI systems translate speech in seconds, eliminating long translation workflows.

##### Global Reach

Content can be distributed to international audiences without language barriers.

##### Lower Costs

Hiring translators or voice actors for every language can be expensive. Many tools now offer free AI audio translation options that allow users to test the technology before upgrading.

##### Scalable Localization

Companies can translate large libraries of content quickly using automation.

### Best Use Cases for AI Voice Translation

AI audio translation is now used across many industries.

- Content Creation

- YouTubers and podcasters translate content for global audiences.

- Business Meetings

- International teams communicate without language barriers.

- Education

- Online courses can be translated into multiple languages.

- Media & Entertainment

- Films, documentaries, and podcasts can be localized faster than traditional dubbing.

Language Support

Solutions such as Fish Audio combine advanced speech recognition with neural voice synthesis to produce natural multilingual audio.

### Free AI Audio Translation Tools

Many platforms now provide free AI audio translation plans or trials.

These options allow users to:

- Test voice translation features

- Evaluate voice quality

- Compare translation accuracy


Free tools are ideal for creators, students, and small teams exploring AI speech translation.

### The Future of AI Speech Translation

AI audio translation technology is evolving quickly.

Future developments may include:

- Real-time multilingual conversations

- Emotion-aware speech synthesis

- Personalized AI voice translation

- Instant video dubbing

- Cross-language virtual assistants


As models become more advanced, speech to speech translation will become even more seamless and human-like.

### Final Thoughts

AI translation technology is transforming how people communicate across languages. With modern AI speech translation systems, users can now convert spoken language into another language instantly using powerful speech to speech translation pipelines.

Tools offering AI voice translation online are making this technology accessible to creators, businesses, and educators worldwide. Many platforms even provide free AI audio translation options so users can experiment before adopting the technology at scale.

Platforms like Fish Audio are helping push the industry forward by combining advanced speech recognition, translation models, and realistic voice synthesis.
As AI continues to improve, AI audio translation will play a key role in breaking down global language barriers and enabling universal communication.

# How to Increase Your Global Audience Reach With Video Translation

Video is a powerful marketing tool that engages, educates, and entertains audiences. It’s no wonder 86 percent of businesses leverage video in their marketing strategy. However, for brands with global…

Video is a powerful marketing tool that engages, educates, and entertains audiences. It’s no wonder [86 percent of businesses](https://blog.hubspot.com/marketing/state-of-video-marketing-new-data) leverage video in their marketing strategy. However, for brands with global ambitions, communicating with foreign audiences via video can pose a linguistic challenge. Your Japanese audience might not “get” the punchline of your joke in English, or your Indonesian audience might have a hard time fully grasping the features of your product if they can’t understand the language. That’s where video translation comes into play.

Video translation in the form of [subtitles](https://www.translate.one/solution/subtitling/) is key to channeling the power of video into growing your global reach. If you want to learn more about subtitle translation, read on. You might be surprised to learn that subtitles can not only expand your international audience but also engage your customers at home. Let’s get started.

## What Is Video Translation?

Video translation is the process of adding subtitles to a video in a language other than the one being used by the actors or [voiceover](https://www.translate.one/solution/voiceover/). For example, if your video features an employee demonstrating the functionality of your product in English, you might use a translation service to add captions in German. From there, you can distribute your subtitled video in Germany and engage German-speaking audiences.

## Where Are Subtitles Most Effective?

When you think of subtitles, your mind might immediately conjure images of foreign television shows and movies. However, there are many more instances in which subtitles are effective. In today’s digital age, subtitles can be added to almost any video, such as:

- Videos on social media, including YouTube videos, Instagram Reels and Stories, TikTok videos, and Facebook videos
- Instructional and educational videos published on your website
- Training videos for employees and other eLearning content
- Paid advertisements that use video on social media and other advertising services

![C:UserseffiesDownloadsHow to Increase Your Global Audience Reach With Video Translation](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

## What Are the Advantages of Adding Subtitles to Videos?

Subtitles are equally as effective when used to engage foreign audiences as they are at capturing and holding the attention of local viewers. One survey found that [69 percent of American consumers](https://www.newtontech.net/en/blog/23083-captions-increase-viewership-accessibility-and-reach) aged 18 to 54 watch videos without sound when in public places. In addition, 25 percent mute videos when in private. So, even if you don’t have grand plans to grow your brand abroad, it’s still worth investing in high-quality captions.

Let’s dig deeper into the benefits of subtitles.

### Reach New Audiences

Subtitles help you reach and engage new audiences in several ways:

- With video translation, people who speak other languages can interact with your content. Subtitles are a cost-effective way of repurposing existing video content for overseas audiences.
- Subtitles ensure your video content is accessible to people with deafness and hearing loss, who make up about [5 percent of the world’s total population](https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/deafness-and-hearing-loss). For more information, read our article [What is Web](https://commit-global.com/what-is-web-accessibility-and-how-to-implement-it/) [Accessibility & How to Implement it?](https://www.translate.one/what-is-web-accessibility-how-to-implement-it/)
- Subtitles capture the interest of those who prefer to watch videos with no sound, which, as we’ve mentioned, is more than half of people in public spaces.

![C:UserseffiesDownloadsHow to Increase Your Global Audience Reach With Video Translation](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

### Stand Out, Capture Attention, and Improve Engagement

More and more marketers are using video to drive brand awareness, strengthen customer relationships, generate leads, and convert. More than [99 percent of current video marketers](https://blog.hubspot.com/marketing/state-of-video-marketing-new-data) will continue using video in the future, with two-thirds planning to maintain or increase their spending. What’s more, 79 percent of marketers who don’t currently use video plan to.

This means consumers will be bombarded with more noise and more clutter, and with more competition in the ring, businesses will have to do more to stand out.

One way to do that is by investing in high-quality, accurate, and authentic captions. Research suggests that as many as [eight in 10 consumers](https://www.newtontech.net/en/blog/23083-captions-increase-viewership-accessibility-and-reach) are more likely to watch a complete video if captions are available.

### Boost Search Engine Optimization

Search engine optimization (SEO) is the process of tweaking content and the technical aspects of a website to increase the likelihood that a web page will rank highly in search engine results for a target keyword.

Video SEO ensures videos are found by people searching for a relevant query. Subtitles play a crucial role, allowing Google and other search engines to index video content and display it in the search results. Videos that cannot be indexed are essentially invisible to searchers.

Videos with captions can bolster your SEO efforts indirectly, too. Search engines reward pages that deliver relevant content, and one metric they use to determine this is the average time a user spends on the page. Engaging videos with subtitles will encourage visitors to stick around for longer, which then signals to search engines that your content is high-quality and worthy of a top-spot ranking.

### Level up Your Return on Investment

https://www.acclaro.com/blog/maximizing-:
![](https://www.acclaro.com/wp-content/uploads/resized/Maximizing-Impact-with-Localized-Video-Your-Complete-Guide-600x0-c-default.png.webp)

Video can significantly boost your ecommerce sales. Case in point: [82 percent of people](https://wyzowl.com/video-marketing-statistics/) say they’ve bought a product or service after watching a brand’s video. However, viewers must understand the content to engage with it. If your videos are only available in English, you’re missing out on 76 percent of the global market.

To reach customers worldwide, you need a [video localization](https://www.acclaro.com/solutions/media-and-production/video-translation-services/) strategy that accounts for the language and cultural preferences of the target audience. [76 percent of online shoppers](https://csa-research.com/Blogs-Events/CSA-in-the-Media/Press-Releases/Consumers-Prefer-their-Own-Language) prefer information in their native language, and a localized video can build audience trust, increase engagement, and drive conversions.

Whether you’re a beginner or want to brush up on best practices, this guide will show you how to create a video localization strategy that maximizes your reach in any market.

## Understanding localized video

So, what is video localization, and how does it differ from video translation? Simply put, it’s the process of [adapting content to account for cultural norms](https://www.acclaro.com/academy/transcreation/) and expectations. Adaptation includes altering the spoken language, visuals, and cultural references to better resonate with the target audience. Unlike video translation, which recreates the message in another language, video localization creates a new, culturally relevant experience.

## Elements of video localization

With this basic definition in mind, let’s break down the six major elements of video localization. These will serve as the building blocks of your strategy.  Please note that a combination of intelligent workflows, continuous localization, and [AI-powered processes](https://www.acclaro.com/blog/the-power-of-ai-to-automate-translation-workflows/) can be used in most stages to speed up delivery, save costs, and optimize quality.

### Language translation

includes translating the original script and on-screen text.

### Cultural adaptation

Involves modifying cultural references, graphics, images, and other aspects of the video to align with local audiences’ norms.

### Regionalization

Adjusts currency, units of measure measurements, dates, and other information to match the target region’s conventions, e.g., metric and imperial or the current currency.

### Subtitles

[Subtitles](https://www.acclaro.com/blog/subtitling-and-dubbing/) are the most affordable way to translate media, but they can be difficult to follow when a video includes important visual details. A mixture of on-screen translated clues can help the user experience.

### Dubbing

Is an effective option for videos with more than one talking head and/or people conversing.

### Voiceover

Is a re-recording of off-screen commentary or narration. It works well for videos with one talking head or an off-screen narrator. This can also be done with synthetic voices. See the Adobe case study for details.

How much you alter a video depends on several factors, including the target market, the type of content, and your budget. For example, you can dub product videos to keep the focus on the demonstrations while also including subtitles for viewers who turn off the sound.

Ultimately, video localization is more art than science as it requires a combination of technical skills, cultural knowledge, and linguistic expertise.

## Why localize video for your business?

As you can see, video localization requires input from multiple stakeholders and professionals. And it can be time-consuming and expensive. You may be wondering if it’s worthwhile for your business. The answer is a resounding yes! Here are just three measurable business benefits:

- Positive brand perception. Localized videos show your commitment to understanding your audience, which improves [customer perception](https://blog.hubspot.com/marketing/what-is-brand-perception) in new markets.
- Higher SEO ranking. Adding localized video titles, descriptions, and tags boosts your video’s search engine optimization (SEO) and visibility.
- Increased engagement. Audiences are more likely to engage with videos that match their cultural norms and preferences.
- Visual explanations. With remote and hybrid workforces, being able to self-service has become key.

For example, our client Adobe Experience Cloud localized its library of educational content and courses to offer global customers a personalized experience in their native language. Users can learn how to maximize the platform’s benefits by accessing over 8,000 videos in nine languages. This allows Adobe to reach a much larger global audience and increase customer engagement with the product.

## Challenges in video localization

So far so good, right? Well, before you dive into video localization, it’s important to understand the challenges involved. Here are four of the biggest obstacles and how to overcome them.

### Consider cultural differences

Adapting content to cultural norms, values, and preferences can be challenging. What is acceptable or humorous in one culture might be offensive or confusing in another. To get it right, you need an in-depth understanding of the local culture. Consider [hiring a localization partne](https://www.acclaro.com/blog/how-to-select-a-language-service-provider-for-successful-global-marketing-campaigns/) r that can help you develop a winning strategy.

### Optimize UI and UX

Each language has its own nuances, idioms, and colloquialisms, which may not exist in the target language. Finding equivalent expressions with the same tone and meaning, whilst adhering to the character count limitations can be very difficult. Jonckers fluid relationship between the engineering team with the translation team enable your design work to shine in all languages.

### Beware of timing and synchronization

Dubbing or subtitles must be synchronized with the original audio. However, different languages have different sentence structures and lengths, which affect timing. Skilled audio and video editors can ensure accurate synchronization.

### Budget appropriately

Video localization is resource-intensive, especially for large-scale projects involving multiple languages. Keeping quality high while adhering to [budget](https://www.acclaro.com/blog/the-cost-of-translation-services-key-factors-and-considerations/) and time constraints is challenging. You can optimize the process with proper planning, streamlined workflows, and technology.

## How to localize your videos: a step-by-step guide

Now that you know the benefits and challenges, let’s walk through how to create a [localization strategy](https://www.acclaro.com/blog/how-can-a-localization-strategy-help-your-business-succeed-globally/). Think of this as your blueprint for launching video content in any market.

### Step 1: Research and planning

Start by researching which international markets offer the greatest potential. You’ll use this information to create a localization plan that includes:

- 1.Target language(s) and location(s)
- 2\. Types of videos
- 3\. Timelines
- 4\. Budget
- 5\. Resources required
- 6\. Metrics for success

### Step 2: Transcription and translation

Transcribe the original audio to create a script. You’ll need the transcript to translate the dialog into the target language.

### Step 3: Subtitling or dubbing

Localize the video through subtitles, dubbing, or both based on local preferences and budget considerations. Here are some ways to do it:

- 1\. Create manual subtitles using free software such as Aegisub or Subtitle Workshop. Applications like these save you money, but the process is time-consuming.

## Introducing the age-old debate: Dubbing vs. Subtitling

The debate between dub vs subtitles is an older one that tends to continue in the future, even between viewers. The main struggle lies in choosing between dub vs sub and which is the best choice to watch the movies made in other languages.

![Dubbing vs Subtitles](https://static-kea.vozo.ai/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/11104238/dubbing-vs-subtitles-1024x581.webp)

Subtitling tends to keep the original audio, and the text is translated to help viewers understand what is being said. Whereas dubbing replaces the original audio, the voiceover is translated into the viewer’s native language. The selection of subbed vs dubbed lies among the audience and their preferences for enjoying the content.

In the article below, we will discuss a detailed comparison between subbing vs dubbing that will help you decide which is the best choice according to the viewing experience.

## Key Differences Between Sub and Dub

The main difference between subtitles and dubbing is how they translate the foreign language content for the viewers. Subtitles translate the text on the screen to let the audience listen to the original audio and read the dialogue. Dubbing translates the entire audio into a new voice in the native language of the viewer.

![A man watching a sci-fi movie with subtitles on a large screen.](https://static-kea.vozo.ai/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/12015610/subtitling-1.webp)

Subtitles are mostly considered as a better choice. They keep the original video intact and are a quick and affordable means for editing a video. However, the viewers have to read the text while they watch the video and some people may find it distracting.

**Read More:**

- [Best Software for Adding Subtitles to Video](https://www.vozo.ai/blogs/best-video-caption-apps)
- [Best Software for Translating Subtitles](https://www.vozo.ai/blogs/best-subtitle-translators)

![A man watching a sci-fi movie with translated dub](<Base64-Image-Removed>)

Dubbing eliminates the context of reading and gives a better experience. Dubbing is suitable for people who prefer audio in their native language. Also, it is expensive and also compromises the originality of the audio.

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Feature** | **Subtitles** | **Dubbing** |
| **Original Audio** | Same as original | Replaced |
| **Authenticity** | Retains original voice and emotion | Does not retain the original voice and emotion |
| **Viewer Action** | Requires reading text | No text reading required |
| **Cost** | Low cost | High cost |
| **Immersion** | Low due to reading | High for people preferring native language audio |
| **Production Time** | Faster | Slow |
| **Lip Sync** | Not needed | Requires keeping the video natural |
| **Accessibility** | Offers less accessibility | Highly accessible |
| **Cultural Nuance** | Retains original expressions | Alters the original meaning |

## Understanding Dubbing: Giving Your Videos a Native Voice

### [What is Dubbing?](https://www.vozo.ai/blogs/what-is-ai-dubbing)

Dubbing consists of completely replacing the audio with a translated version in the viewer’s native language. Dubbing consists of re-recording the dialogue in the required language and using voice actors to match the lip movement of the original. Some of the aspects involved in dubbing comprise the following:

- **Translation:** The original dialogues are translated into a new language, taking care of the lip sync and cultural nuances.
- **Voice Casting:** Voice actors are considered for dubbing whose voice best matches the tone of the original speaker.
- **Recording:** The voice actors have to record the script in the studio looking at the video to match the lip movements and emotion.
- **Mixing**: The new track is mixed with the original effects of sound so that natural audio is achieved.

So, What Exactly is Dubbing?

Think of it as a voice swap for videos. The original dialogue is replaced with a new version in a different language, so you can watch without reading.

The Dubbing Process

1\. Transcription

Getting the original words down.

2\. Translation

Adapting the script to a new language.

3\. Voiceover

Recording the new voices.

4\. Mixing & Syncing

Putting it all together.

The Upsides

More Immersive

Lets you get lost in the story without the distraction of reading.

Wider Audience

Opens up content to kids and people with visual impairments.

Stronger Emotions

Hearing feelings expressed in your own language can be more powerful.

The Challenges

Costly and Slow

It’s a big production that needs more time and money than subtitles.

Tough to Lip-Sync

If the new words don’t match the actors’ mouths, it looks awkward.

Loses Originality

You don’t get to hear the original performance from the actors.

### Advantages of Dubbing

Dubbing offers various advantages to the viewers, and some of these include the following:

#### 1.Immersive Native Language Experience

When a viewer hears a dialogue in their native language, they are more engaged with the visuals with minimal distraction and enhancing the immersion.

#### 2.Broader Audience Coverage

Dubbing offers accessibility to people who find reading subtitles difficult, such as people with visual impairments or children.

#### 3.Natural Viewing

If dubbing is of high quality, the viewers can enjoy the content without reading.

#### 4.Stronger Resonance for Emotions

When emotions are expressed in the native language, it can create a very deep connection between the characters and the audience.

### The Challenges of Dubbing

Dubbing comes with many advantages, but it also has some challenges. Some of these challenges that you may encounter are as follows:

#### 1.Time-Consuming and Needs Resources

Dubbing requires a very extensive effort involving directors, translators, sound engineers and voice actors. Such effort takes more time and is costly compared to subtitling.

#### 2.Challenge of Lip Sync

Lip Sync is a major challenge when it comes to dubbing the video. The movement of the actors may be difficult to match. Hence, when it is done poorly, it can cause a lot of distraction and impact the immersion expected from dubbing.

#### 3.Dilemma of Voice Match

Matching the voice, emotion and tone with the original actor is highly challenging. It plays a vital role in maintaining the impact of the story.

#### 4.Authenticity Trade-Off

Dubbing compromises the cultural richness and original content, disconnecting viewers from the authenti
