# The Best AI Voiceover Tools in 2026 (Tried, Tested & Ranked)

[Jul 17, 2025](https://www.youngurbanproject.com/best-ai-voiceover-tools/)

—

by

[Sakshi Jonwal](https://www.youngurbanproject.com/author/sakshijonwal/)

in [Marketing Software](https://www.youngurbanproject.com/category/marketing-software/)

Let’s be honest, voiceovers can be a huge pain.

You’ve got to find the right voice actor, send scripts back and forth, maybe go through a few awkward revisions, and if you’re on a deadline? Good luck.

But in the last couple of years, something’s changed. A new wave of **AI voiceover tools** has started replacing that entire process, and not in a cheap, robotic way either. These tools are starting to sound… kind of real. In some cases, eerily real.

And for content creators, marketers, educators, and pretty much anyone making videos or audio content, it’s opened up a whole new world of speed, flexibility, and reach.

Table of Contents

Toggle

## **So, What Exactly** **_Are_** **AI Voiceover Tools?**

In simple terms, AI voiceover tools turn written text into spoken words. But not like those stiff, outdated text-to-speech bots we all remember from the early internet. No, these tools use some serious AI tech, like deep learning models and neural networks, to replicate human voices with surprising accuracy.

You type. The software speaks. And in most cases, it actually _sounds_ like a real person.

What’s even more wild is that some of these tools let you **clone your own voice**. You record a few samples, and after that, you can generate any script in your own tone, accent, and pacing, without opening your mouth again. It’s both fascinating and slightly freaky.

## **How Do AI Voice Generators Actually Work?**

Alright, quick breakdown, without diving too deep into the tech jungle.

![How Do AI Voice Generators Actually Work](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20675%20844'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

At the heart of these tools is something called **text-to-speech**, or TTS. Combine that with **deep learning**, and you get software that doesn’t just read your words, it tries to understand how a human would _say_ them.

That means natural pauses, emotion, rhythm, and tone. Some tools can even add laughter, sighs, or different speaking styles (like casual vs. formal). It’s not perfect across the board, but honestly? Some of the results are better than amateur voice actors.

And like I mentioned earlier, **voice cloning** is becoming a big deal. Tools like ElevenLabs or Descript let you feed in audio samples, and then they basically build a digital version of your voice.

Imagine turning an entire blog post into a podcast in _your voice_, without recording a thing. That’s not science fiction anymore.

## **Why Creators Are Switching to AI Voiceover Tools in 2026**

There are a few big reasons creators are leaning into this tech, especially now that the quality has caught up to the hype.

### **1\. You Save Time and Money. A Lot of It.**

Hiring a voiceover artist takes time. And if you’re on a budget, it gets expensive, fast. AI voice generators let you skip all of that. Just input your text, choose a voice, and you’ve got a clean, professional audio file in minutes.

And if you need to make changes? No problem. No awkward re-recordings or coordination needed.

### **2\. You Can Scale Content Without Burning Out**

If you’re a YouTuber, online educator, or marketer producing content regularly, you know how hard it is to keep up. AI tools make it easy to pump out narrations for multiple projects without sacrificing quality (or losing your voice).

This is especially useful for creators managing content across YouTube, Reels, podcasts, blog videos, you name it.

### **3\. Multilingual Options Open Up Global Reach**

A lot of AI voice tools now support dozens, even over 100, languages and accents. Want to turn your English course into a Spanish or Hindi version? You can do that. It’s a huge win for anyone looking to grow internationally without having to hire translators or multilingual voice talent.

### **4\. Consistent Quality Every Time**

Let’s be real, even the best human voice actors have off days. With AI, you get the same tone, delivery, and pacing every single time. That kind of consistency is gold when you’re trying to maintain a certain brand sound or voice across platforms.

## **The Top 10 AI Voiceover Tools of 202** 6

Alright, let’s get into the good stuff. There are a lot of tools out there, but here are ten that are really standing out this year, each with its own strengths depending on what you’re trying to do.

### **1\. Fiverr Go – Best for Hybrid AI + Human Voiceovers**

This one’s interesting because it blends tech with a human touch. Fiverr Go lets you generate AI voiceovers, but then it pairs them with professional human editors who polish the final product. It’s ideal for creators who want the speed of AI but don’t want to lose the warmth and polish of a human voice.

Think ad agencies, branded content, promo videos, anywhere tone matters.

### **2\. ElevenLabs – Best for Voice Cloning and Audiobooks**

This tool has been making waves because of how **real** its voices sound. It also gives you advanced controls for emotion, tone, and pacing, which makes it perfect for audiobooks, storytelling, or any kind of long-form narration.

You can even create your own synthetic voice model and use it across projects. Honestly, if you’re serious about audio, [ElevenLabs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ElevenLabs) is a must-try.

### **3\. Freepik – Best for All-in-One Audio and Video Creation**

**Freepik AI Suite** is an all-in-one creative platform that covers image generation, video creation, editing, and a full stock library. On top of that, it also includes a solid set of audio tools: Voice Generator, Voice Cloning, Voice Changer, Sound Effects Generator, and Music Generator.

The Voice Generator includes models from ElevenLabs and Gemini-TTS for multi-speaker synthesis. You can filter voices by language, gender, and accent, then fine-tune speed, pitch, and pauses. Voice Cloning lets you train a model on your own samples so you can generate scripts in your voice without recording. And Voice Changer lets you upload any audio or video file and swap the voice using a reference, great for repurposing existing content.

But what really sets it apart is how everything connects. You can generate a video with the AI Video Generator, add a voiceover, apply Lip Sync to match the audio to on-screen faces, layer in sound effects or custom music, and handle it all without leaving the platform.

The [Freepik AI Voice Generator](https://www.freepik.com/) runs on a credit-based system with limited free access to get started.

### **4\. Murf.ai – Best for Script Control and Editing Flexibility**

Murf feels more like a mini production suite than just a voice tool. It gives you options to adjust pitch, speed, add pauses, and fine-tune your script. There’s even support for team collaboration, which is perfect if you’re working with clients or a small content team.

You also get access to a library of royalty-free background music. Solid pick for anyone doing e-learning, training videos, or YouTube tutorials.

### **5\. Fliki – Best for Turning Blog Posts Into Voice-Over Videos**

This one’s made for content marketers. If you’ve got a blog post or script, Fliki turns it into a video with AI narration, visuals, and music in just a few clicks. It’s not going to replace a full video editor, but for repurposing written content into something visual and engaging? It’s fantastic.

# How to Lip Sync Video: The Complete Guide to AI-Powered Video Lip Synchronization

_Transform any video with perfect lip sync using cutting-edge AI technology_

* * *

![How to Lip Sync Video](https://lipsync.studio/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstore.lipsync.studio%2Fblog_images%2Fhow-to-lip-sync-video.png&w=1920&q=75)

## What is Video Lip Sync?

Video lip sync (also known as lip-syncing or audio dubbing) is the process of synchronizing a person's lip movements in a video with a different audio track. This technology has revolutionized content creation, enabling filmmakers, marketers, educators, and social media creators to:

- **Dub videos into different languages** while maintaining natural lip movements
- **Replace poor audio quality** with professional voice recordings
- **Create engaging content** where characters speak with any voice
- **Produce multilingual marketing videos** without reshooting

With advances in artificial intelligence, what once required expensive studios and manual rotoscoping can now be done in minutes using AI-powered tools.

* * *

## Why Use AI for Video Lip Sync?

Traditional lip sync methods are incredibly time-consuming and require extensive manual work. AI lip sync technology offers several advantages:

| Traditional Method | AI-Powered Method |
| --- | --- |
| Hours of manual editing | Processed in minutes |
| Requires skilled animators | No technical skills needed |
| Expensive studio costs | Affordable and accessible |
| Limited quality | Photorealistic results |
| Difficult to scale | Process multiple videos easily |

* * *

## Step-by-Step Guide: How to Lip Sync Video with AI

### Step 1: Prepare Your Source Video

Before you begin, ensure your source video meets these requirements:

- **Clear face visibility**: The subject's face should be clearly visible and well-lit
- **Frontal or slight angle**: While our AI can process faces from various angles, **front-facing subjects produce the best results**. Side profiles and partial views are supported but may have reduced accuracy
- **Resolution**: We support videos from 360p all the way up to **4K Ultra HD resolution** for the highest quality output
- **Duration**: Most AI tools support videos up to 10 minutes
- **Format**: Common formats like MP4, MOV, or AVI

> ⚠️ **Important**: Avoid using videos with **embedded subtitles or text overlays**. The AI may distort or remove text areas during lip sync generation because it cannot distinguish subtitles from regular video content. For best results, use clean videos without any on-screen text.

> **Pro Tip**: Videos with minimal camera movement and consistent lighting produce the best results.

### Supported Character Types

Our AI lip sync technology is incredibly versatile and works with a wide variety of subjects:

- 👤 **Real Humans**: Natural, photorealistic lip sync for live-action footage
- 🎨 **Anime & Animation**: Perfect synchronization for 2D and 3D animated characters
- 🐱 **Animals**: Yes, we can make your pets and animal footage talk!
- 🤖 **Any Character with a Mouth**: From puppets to mascots, fantasy creatures to cartoon characters — if it has lips or a mouth, our AI can sync it!

This versatility makes LipSync Studio the ultimate all-in-one solution for any lip sync project, regardless of your content type.

### Step 2: Prepare Your Audio

Your replacement audio is crucial for a convincing lip sync:

- **Quality**: Use clear, high-quality audio recordings
- **Language**: Works with any language
- **Voice type**: Can be your own voice, AI-generated voice, or any recorded audio
- **Format**: MP3, WAV, M4A, or other common audio formats

**Audio Sources You Can Use:**

1. **Voice Recording**: Record your own voice
2. **Text-to-Speech (TTS)**: Generate speech from text using AI voices
3. **Voice Cloning**: Clone any voice to speak your script
4. **Music & Songs**: Yes, you can even make people sing!

### Step 3: Upload to an AI Lip Sync Tool

Using **LipSync Studio's Video Lip Sync** feature (powered by the InfiniteTalkVideo model):

1. Navigate to the Video Lip Sync tool
2. **Upload your video**: Drag and drop or click to select your source video
3. **Add your audio**: Upload your audio file or generate one using TTS
4. **Optional**: Add a mask image if you want to control which characters speak
5. **Set resolution**: Choose from 360p up to 4K based on your needs
6. **Click Generate**: The AI will process your video

### Step 4: Review and Download

Once processing is complete:

- Preview the generated video
- Check lip synchronization accuracy
- Download in your preferred format
- Share or use in your projects

* * *

## Advanced Features for Professional Results

### Using Mask Images for Multi-Person Videos

When your video contains multiple people but you only want one person to speak:

1. Create a black-and-white mask image
2. **White areas**: People who should speak (lips will be synced)
3. **Black areas**: People who should remain silent
4. Upload the mask along with your video

This is perfect for:

- Interviews where only one person speaks at a time
- Group videos with a designated speaker
- Selective dubbing in crowd scenes

### Resolution and Quality Settings

| Resolution | Best For | Credit Cost |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 360p | Quick previews, social media stories | Lowest |
| 480p | Standard web video | Low |
| 720p | YouTube, presentations | Medium |
| 1080p | Professional content | Higher |
| 2K/4K | High-end production | Highest |

### Prompt Customization

Use prompts to guide the AI generation:

```
Example prompt: "A person with natural expression speaking clearly.
Minimal head movement. Eyes looking at camera.
Natural blinking pattern."
```

* * *

## Common Use Cases for Video Lip Sync

### 1\. Content Localization

Translate your videos into any language while keeping the speaker's face in sync:

- Educational content for global audiences
- Marketing videos for international markets
- Entertainment media dubbing

### 2\. Voice-Over Replacement

Replace existing audio without reshooting:

- Fix audio quality issues
- Change voice talent after filming
- Add professional narration

### 3\. Accessibility

Create content for hearing-impaired audiences:

- Add sign language interpreters
- Create visual speech aids

### 4\. Creative Content

- Make historical figures "speak"
- Create viral social media content
- Produce entertaining parodies

* * *

## Best Practices for Perfect Lip Sync

### ✅ Do:

- Use high-quality source videos with clear facial visibility
- Match audio timing roughly to the video length
- Use natural speech patterns in your audio
- Start with shorter clips to test quality
- Use consistent lighting in source video

### ❌ Don't:

- Use heavily compressed or pixelated videos
- Choose videos with covered faces or masks
- Use audio with long pauses or unnatural pacing
- Expect perfect results with extreme face angles
- Process videos longer than supported duration

* * *

## Comparing Video Lip Sync Models

At LipSync Studio, we offer multiple models for different needs:

| Model | Input | Best For | Max Duration |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Video Lip Sync** | Video + Audio | Existing videos, dubbing | 10 minutes |
| **Image Lip Sync** | Image + Audio | Creating talking avatars | 500 seconds |
| **Multi-Speaker** | Image + 2 Audio | Podcasts, dialogues | 500 seconds |

* * *

## Frequently Asked Questions

### How long does video lip sync take?

Processing time depends on video length and resolution. A 1-minute video at 720p typically takes 10-15 minutes.

### What languages are supported?

AI lip sync works with any language! The AI analyzes the audio phonemes and matches them to lip movements.

### Can I lip sync with singing?

Yes! You can sync videos to singing audio, music, or any vocal performance.

### Is the result realistic?

Modern AI produces highly realistic results, especially with good quality source material. The technology continues to improve rapidly.

To opt out of being tracked by Google Analytics across all websites, visit [http://tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout](http://tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout).

In 2025, video content will continue to dominate the digital landscape. Whether you’re an educator, content creator, or marketer, optimizing your videos for search engines is decisive. This comprehensive guide will help you understand and implement the latest Video SEO strategies to improve visibility, drive organic traffic, and establish topical authority.

## **What Is Video SEO?**

Video SEO/Optimization is the process of boosting your video content to grow its visibility organically in search results.The objective is to make your videos appear significantly in search results when users are looking for any relevant topics you have covered in video.

By applying video [**SEO optimization**](https://www.proceedinnovative.com/blog/local-vs-organic-seo/) best practices, it will have more probability that users will find your video on top and click on it instead of other competitor’s video.

When done correctly, video SEO can lead to:

- Expanded viewer base
- Achieved subscriber growth
- Amplified channel growth
- Strengthened audience engagement
- Increased conversions and user trust

So, how exactly do you get your videos to rank on Google? And what are the top approaches you should be utilizing?

Let’s dive in.

## 1\. Making Videos on Trending Topics – How to do that?

Even if your video ranks high, it won’t get many views if no one is searching for the topic.

That’s why choosing the _right_ topic — one people are actively looking for — is essential. But how do you find these topics?

Let’s say you run a channel focused on fitness and wellness.

Let’s assume keywords like   **“fitness tip”** or **“workout routine”** into a keyword research tool like **Moz** or **SEMrush**.

![semrush keyword finder](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%20886%20646%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)

At first, the tool might return to a wide range of search query. But many of them may be too broad or not directly aligned with your content.

### **Narrow It Down**

To get more relevant results, ask yourself:

What kind of fitness content do you create? Home workouts? Gym-based training? Yoga or HIIT?

When users search for workouts, they’re usually looking for specific goals or formats, such as:

🏋️ _“Easy 30-minute home workout”_

🧘 _“morning yoga for beginners”_

🔥 _“30 Days workout to burn fat”_

To drill down on these, use the **“Include keywords” filter** in your tool.

Select **“Any keywords”**, and add phrases like:

- “At home”
- “No equipment”
- “For beginners”
- “Fat burning”
- “instructor”

Now you’ve got a tighter, high-intent list of content ideas your audience is actively searching for.

![semrush keywords list](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%20684%20650%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)

Keyword Magic Tool can help you narrow your list to only show topics where videos appear in search results.

Just toggle these options under “ **SERP features**” in the “ **Advanced filters**” drop-down:

![Semrush keyword tool](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%201024%20523%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)

## 2\. How to Create High-Quality Videos

So how do you want to promote your product or services on google? That’s the main question. Google favors high quality content—but what does “quality” mean?

Let’s take an example, a cooking specialist or chef focuses on clear, step-by-step instructions and taking some shots, while a fitness creator should prioritize demonstrating some training and tricks. Similarly, a travel vlogger should focus on real visuals and storytelling to move viewers to new destinations.

That said, most high-quality videos share a few common traits:

- **A strong start.** Great videos knob viewers in the first around 15–20 seconds, high chances to stick throughout the video.
- **Real value.** Whether educating, entertaining, encouraging, or solving a problem, high-quality videos give a reason to viewers to find and watch video.
- **Solid creation.** Good graphics, clear sounds, and clean editing all contribute to a more immersive, entertaining experience.

In short, value quality is a mix of content and execution. The more engaging and enhanced your videos are, the more likely people are to pay attention.

# A Guide to Mastering Multilingual Compliance in Global Ops

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6501980675e203b46e89f4ad/685919cd3e556f269b84375d_6645bc61302f51cc71f928c4_Blog-header%2520v05-20240426%2520(57).webp)

In an increasingly interconnected global marketplace, financial institutions have the task of navigating a complex web of regulations, each unique to its jurisdiction. This complexity amplifies when the institutions operate across borders, dealing with a multitude of languages and cultural contexts. This brings us to a crucial aspect of international business operations - multilingual compliance. But what exactly does it mean?

Multilingual compliance is the ability of an organization to align its operations with the legal and regulatory requirements of different regions, taking into account the diverse languages, cultural nuances, and unique legal frameworks. This includes understanding and adhering to laws relating to data privacy, financial transactions, taxation, and anti-money laundering (AML), among others, in multiple languages.

Mastering multilingual compliance is a significant undertaking, but it's of paramount importance. Global operations involve not just language translations but also demand deep understanding of varying regulatory landscapes, which when ignored or misunderstood, can lead to significant legal and financial repercussions. Therefore, effective compliance strategies are not just about following rules. They form the foundation of trustworthy relationships with customers, partners, and regulatory authorities across the world.

The digital revolution has made the world smaller, but it has simultaneously made the regulatory world much larger. How do you, as a financial institution, ensure that your business remains on the right side of the law, no matter where your operations extend? How do you decode the legal jargon in various languages and align your practices accordingly? This definitive guide aims to provide you with the necessary insights and strategies to master multilingual compliance in global operations, helping you successfully navigate the international compliance landscape.

## **The challenge of multilingual compliance in global operations**

The essence of operating globally lies in the ability to effectively navigate the intricate maze of diverse regulations, languages, and cultural norms. Multilingual compliance, therefore, isn't merely about translating legal texts. It demands an understanding of regulatory nuances and subtleties embedded in various cultures and languages.

At the heart of multilingual compliance challenges are distinct laws and regulations across jurisdictions. From [anti-money laundering (AML)](https://flagright.com/maintaining-aml-and-regulatory-compliance) to [data privacy](https://flagright.com/data-privacy-and-security-in-aml-compliance) norms, different countries have diverse frameworks with their own sets of rules. This regulatory diversity presents a constant hurdle for financial institutions, as they must align their operations with the unique compliance requirements of every region in which they operate.

Moreover, laws and regulations aren't static. They evolve over time, driven by changes in government, policy shifts, technological advancements, or societal factors. Thus, staying current with such dynamic regulatory landscapes across multiple jurisdictions becomes a formidable task.

Then there's the language barrier. Legal and financial terminologies often lose or shift meaning during translation, creating room for misinterpretation. Thus, accurately translating and understanding complex legal documents, contracts, and regulatory guidelines in multiple languages is a massive undertaking. It's not just about what is said, but also how it's said. The same phrase might convey different meanings in different cultures.

These intricacies are compounded by cultural differences. Each region has its unique cultural context, which influences the interpretation and application of laws. For instance, the concept of privacy varies widely across cultures, influencing data privacy laws differently. Understanding these cultural nuances is a non-negotiable aspect of effective multilingual compliance.

All these challenges underscore the need for specialized skills, resources, and advanced tools to master multilingual compliance in global operations. The process may be complex, but it's not insurmountable. The key lies in the strategic blend of technological innovations, expert collaborations, continuous learning, and a robust compliance framework.

## **The importance of cultural training and hiring**

While laws and regulations form the backbone of compliance, understanding the cultural contexts within which these rules operate is equally crucial for mastering multilingual compliance. This is where cultural training and hiring local expertise play a pivotal role.

Cultural training involves educating employees about the nuances, beliefs, and behaviors of different cultures. When it comes to compliance, these cultural elements can significantly influence how regulations are interpreted and applied. For example, the approach to customer service varies widely from one culture to another, impacting how institutions handle customer data and privacy.

Training programs can help employees understand these cultural nuances, fostering more effective communication and better decision-making in the compliance process. Such training can range from understanding local business etiquette and negotiation styles to interpreting the cultural underpinnings of legal terminology.

However, cultural training is just one piece of the puzzle. It needs to be complemented by the insights and knowledge that come from hiring local experts. This is particularly important in areas where language and cultural barriers could lead to misinterpretations of regulatory requirements.

Our modes of communication have evolved significantly in these last few years. COVID-19 has increased the use of video in our daily lives. The use of the internet has also reached new highs.

[90% of consumers](https://blog.hubspot.com/service/customer-service-stats) in the USA consider a company's customer service before purchasing. [After just one lousy service interaction, 60%](https://cloudblogs.microsoft.com/dynamics365/bdm/2016/07/22/4-frustrating-customer-service-experiences-and-how-to-fix-them/) will no longer buy from a company.

It's time to shift your approach to customer service. Videos are sought-after content formats now. So it's high time to add videos on customer service.

This article will cover everything about adding videos to your customer service.

## What is video customer service?

Undoubtedly, excellent customer service can take your business to a new level. Again, find out ways to level up your existing customer support. Adding video to support customers is a hassle-free way to communicate with customers. It also improves brand value.

## How to add videos on customer service

If you are clueless about how and where to add videos, here is your guide!

### Onboard customers with a video guide

Get ready to bid goodbye to text-heavy guides. Welcome them with an engaging onboarding process. Use them to introduce potential customers in an informative and visually pleasing way. Help them get started with your services and educate them through the process.

With [Xero](https://www.xero.com/), business owners have 24/7 access to financial data, banking, and expert advice. Xero's ["Getting Started"](https://tv.xero.com/detail/video/5298044970001/getting-started-with-xero) video is a terrific video guide. It becomes a no-brainer for new users.

### Add product demos

[60% of consumers](https://invideo.io/blog/video-marketing-statistics/) would rather watch a brief video than read the text about a brand's products or services. Take advantage of this opportunity to wow your customers by adding a demo on the customer service page.

What they do is show you how to use a product. Show the products to potential buyers. Help your customers to understand the product.

[Airtable'](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=p1-0QVWa5ZU) s demo video is a perfect example if you want to add customer support videos.

### Shoot personalized video

For online shoppers, borders are no obstacle these days. Getting their attention, however, has one caveat: you have to speak their language.

The task of translating and localizing your marketing content may be daunting, but knowing the right tools and approaches can alleviate some of your concerns. That’s why I created this guide: to provide you with **concrete tips, real examples, and a list of tools** to set you up for success in marketing localization.

## What is marketing localization?

![What is marketing localization](https://webflow-assets2.redokun.com/615dc53ac3f5ddb2f90d1117/66bdcc2ee6681922b82eb45d_62b439fa0a3f1b4d65eae43c_marketing-localization.jpeg)

Marketing localization refers to **designing or adapting marketing campaigns in accordance with the target audience’s language, culture, and consumer behaviors.** It is one of the major elements of your [localization strategy](https://redokun.com/blog/localization-strategy).

When you localize your brand assets, you craft marketing messages to suit local preferences for the following:

- **Language:** What are the major languages spoken in the target market? What about region-specific dialects?
- **Tone of voice and messaging:** Would your target audience prefer sales talk or a more toned-down approach? What trends are capturing the audience, and how can you leverage them to make your brand feel more relevant?
- **Visuals, design, and colors:** What color schemes, layouts, and images appeal to the audience?
- **Distribution channels:** What channels should you focus on to reach the audience? For example, do they use Facebook, VKontakte, Naver, or Weibo?
- **Content types**: Are they more likely to engage with short- or long-form content?
- **Price structure**: Should their [purchasing power](https://smallbusiness.chron.com/consumer-buying-power-68682.html) affect how you set prices?

As you can see, language is essential, but that’s not all there is to it. Brand localization needs to be integrated with the overall marketing goals of your company to ensure a seamless customer experience.

**That means you should consider localization in every aspect of your marketing mix, from product development to promotion.**

That does _not_ mean hyper-localization is the answer for everyone. Too much standardization means you won’t stand out, but heavy focus on adapting to every target market is not scalable and may break your budget.

> In-depth market research, analysis, and goal-setting based on your [localization KPIs](https://redokun.com/blog/localization-strategy#toc-3) will go far in helping you decide where to strike a balance between localization and standardization.

{{banner-component}}

### Marketing localization vs. localized marketing

![Marketing localization vs. localized marketing](https://webflow-assets2.redokun.com/615dc53ac3f5ddb2f90d1117/66bdcc2ee6681922b82eb45a_62b43a1811144de30d92c666_marketing-localization-vs-localized-marketing.jpeg)

They may sound like the same thing, but marketing localization and localization marketing are actually very different.

Marketing localization is the **_process_ of adapting marketing campaigns to other markets.** The outputs will include, but not be limited to

- Translations of advertising copy that focus on nuanced adjustments
- UX design that ensures multilingual accessibility across touch points
- Budgets that allocate resources according to locale priorities
- Campaign modifications that follow local laws and regulations

On the other hand, localization marketing is the **_strategy_ that** **marketing teams may pursue as part of international expansion efforts.** The strategy will integrate localization _processes_ with types of marketing used by your business, such as

- Digital marketing
- Inbound marketing
- Content marketing
- Search engine marketing
- Social media marketing

Basically, you can add ‘localization’ to any of the marketing types above to describe the process by which they are localized. Digital marketing localization, for example, is how you adapt digital content assets like website copy, blog posts, and social media to fit linguistic and cultural contexts.

# The Multi-Format Magic of Microlearning Platforms: Video, Text, and Interactive Training Success

![Nikita Chopra](https://app.hubspot.com/settings/avatar/d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e)

[Nikita Chopra](https://www.5mins.ai/resources/blog/author/nikita-chopra)

Microlearning platforms are redefining corporate training by leveraging diverse content formats to cater to different learning styles. Combining video, text, and interactive elements creates a holistic training approach that drives engagement, retention, and performance.

This blog explores how incorporating multiple formats into your **microlearning training** strategy can maximize learning outcomes for employees while aligning with business goals.

### **Why Multi-Format Microlearning Works**

Different learners absorb information in different ways. By using a variety of formats, microlearning platforms ensure that content is accessible, engaging, and effective for all employees.

**Key Benefits of Multi-Format Microlearning:**

**1\. Increased Engagement:** Diverse content types keep learners interested and motivated.

**2\. Improved Retention:** Visual, auditory, and interactive elements reinforce knowledge in multiple ways. You can dive deeper into how microlearning combats the forgetting curve [here](https://www.5mins.ai/resources/blog/draftthe-science-behind-bite-sized-learning-how-microlearning-enhances-knowledge-retention?hsLang=en)

**3\. Catered Learning Styles:** Accommodates visual, auditory, and kinesthetic learners.

**4\. Enhanced Accessibility:** Mobile-friendly formats ensure content is available anytime, anywhere.

* * *

### **Breaking Down the Formats: Video, Text, and Interactive Content**

**1\. Video Microlearning: Engaging Visual Content**

Videos are a cornerstone of microlearning platforms, offering visually engaging and concise explanations of complex topics.

**Why Video Works:**

- Simplifies complex concepts with visuals and animations.
- Appeals to visual and auditory learners simultaneously.
- Easy to consume on mobile devices, making it ideal for on-the-go learning.

[Learn how AI-powered interactive videos are shaping modern training](https://www.5mins.ai/resources/blog/interactive-video-in-customer-education-training-transforming-the-learning-experience?hsLang=en)

**Example:** A 3-minute explainer video on cybersecurity best practices with animated visuals and a voiceover to guide learners.

**Best Practices for Video Microlearning:**

- Keep videos under 5 minutes for maximum engagement.
- Use subtitles to enhance accessibility.
- Include a clear call-to-action or follow-up activity.

* * *

2\. Text-Based Microlearning: Quick and Clear Knowledge Sharing

Text content offers a simple, effective way to share information that employees can revisit anytime.

**Why Text Works:**

- Ideal for learners who prefer reading and note-taking.
- Allows for in-depth exploration of topics at the learner's own pace.
- Can be easily integrated into emails, PDFs, or in-platform modules.

**Example:** A quick reference guide outlining the steps to file an expense report, included as a downloadable resource.

**Best Practices for Text Content:**

- Use bullet points and headers for readability.
- Highlight key points with bold text or callout boxes.
- Combine with quizzes or infographics for reinforcement.

* * *

**3\. Interactive Microlearning: Hands-On Engagement**

Interactive content allows learners to actively participate in the learning process, enhancing comprehension and retention.

**Why Interaction Works:**

- Encourages active learning through quizzes, simulations, and branching scenarios.
- Provides immediate feedback, reinforcing correct behaviors.
- Builds practical skills through real-world applications.

[Explore proven strategies for gamified, scenario-based learning in our guide](https://www.5mins.ai/resources/blog/7-proven-strategies-to-create-engaging-and-effective-microlearning-content-for-e-learning?hsLang=en)

**Example:** A role-playing simulation where a sales rep practices handling customer objections and receives instant feedback.

**Best Practices for Interactive Microlearning:**

- Incorporate gamified elements like points, badges, or leaderboards.
- Use scenario-based learning to mimic real-life challenges.
- Ensure interactions are intuitive and mobile-friendly.

* * *

### **How to Combine Formats for Maximum Impact**

1. **Blend Formats Strategically:** Use videos for overviews, text for detailed information, and interactive elements for practice.
2. **Tailor Content to Roles:** Create format-specific modules based on job requirements and learning preferences.
3. **Leverage Analytics:** Use platform data to identify which formats resonate most with learners and optimize accordingly.
4. **Ensure Cohesion:** Design modules that flow seamlessly from one format to another for a cohesive learning experience.

### **Why HR Leaders Should Embrace Multi-Format Microlearning**

1. **Engages Diverse Teams:** Accommodates different learning preferences across the workforce.
2. **Improves ROI:** Higher engagement and retention rates translate to better business outcomes.

For more insight on cost-effectiveness and reduced training time, see [our post](https://www.5mins.ai/resources/blog/bite-sized-learning-for-millennials-and-gen-z-embracing-microlearning-for-the-modern-workforce?hsLang=en) about bite-sized learning for modern workforces.
3. **Drives Continuous Learning:** Keeps employees motivated to upskill with varied, interesting content.

* * *

### **Conclusion: Unlocking the Power of Multi-Format Microlearning**

Microlearning platforms that combine video, text, and interactive elements deliver unmatched flexibility and effectiveness.

By leveraging multiple formats, organizations can create dynamic training experiences that meet employees where they are, driving both engagement and measurable results.

# WHAT IS MULTILINGUAL TESTIMONIAL PRODUCTION?

In today’s increasingly globalized marketplace, leveraging multilingual testimonial production has become a vital video marketing strategy. Testimonials play a crucial role in building trust and credibility, but when your audience speaks various languages, it’s important to adapt. Multilingual testimonial production allows brands to connect authentically with diverse viewers by delivering customer stories in multiple languages, ensuring messages resonate deeply across borders.

## DIVING DEEPER: THE MULTILINGUAL TESTIMONIAL PROCESS

Multilingual testimonial production involves creating customer stories in several languages to connect with an international and culturally diverse audience. This approach demonstrates a brand’s respect for inclusivity and its awareness of cultural differences, which helps you build genuine connections with viewers from a range of backgrounds. Adapting testimonial content into multiple languages not only increases accessibility but also shows a clear commitment to valuing all customers, no matter their location or language preference.

When you deliver testimonials in various languages, you foster a sense of belonging among clients who may otherwise feel excluded. This strategy naturally encourages broader audience engagement and leads to stronger, longer-lasting relationships.

Another benefit of creating multilingual testimonials is the positive perception it generates for your brand’s image. Customers trust companies that go the extra mile to communicate in their native language, especially when it comes to personal, relatable stories. This trust translates to higher retention and improved brand loyalty. By localizing testimonials, you remove barriers to understanding and make your content more appealing. As a result, you stand out in a crowded digital environment, giving your brand a competitive edge.

### STRATEGIC VALUE OF MULTILINGUAL CUSTOMER STORIES

Incorporating testimonials in different languages into your video content strategy offers substantial advantages. First, it allows you to enter new international markets more effectively, as audiences are much more likely to engage with messages tailored to their own language and culture.

Second, multilingual testimonials increase audience trust because viewers see real customers with similar backgrounds and stories, making the message more relatable and believable. Third, this approach enables you to demonstrate your brand’s commitment not just to diversity, but to genuine customer care, which is increasingly important in today’s business landscape.

Focusing on international engagement also helps optimize your brand’s online visibility. With multilingual testimonial content, your resources become discoverable by search engines in multiple languages, increasing organic global reach. This translates into better performance metrics, including increased views, higher retention times, and ultimately, stronger conversion rates. Brands that prioritize authenticity and inclusivity through localized content are well-positioned to succeed and grow in diverse markets.

## GETTING STARTED WITH MULTILINGUAL TESTIMONIAL VIDEOS

![filmmakers](https://cdn.beverlyboy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/25004334/Scene-Switcher-Easy-Green-Screen-Tricks-Using-Your-iPhone-2025-06-04T193410.884Z-output1.png)

To create impactful multilingual testimonial videos, start by identifying which languages are most relevant for your primary and potential audiences. Analyze your customer base and determine the top regions and demographics where your products or services are gaining traction.

Next, source native speakers or satisfied customers who can provide authentic, culturally nuanced testimonials. Their comfort and fluency will help your video resonate with viewers and avoid any awkwardness or miscommunication that can occur with non-native speakers.

Once you’ve captured the raw testimonials, professionally translate and localize the content rather than relying on basic translation tools. This ensures that idioms, humor, and tone remain natural in each language version. If shooting separate videos becomes impractical, integrate high-quality subtitles or voice-overs to help audiences follow along. Always align visual elements and branding across films, ensuring that color, style, and messaging reflect your company’s identity while remaining sensitive to cultural distinctions.

• Choose relevant target languages after thorough research.

• Work with native-speaking customers or actors for authenticity.

• Rely on experienced translators and localization specialists.

• Use professional subtitles or voice-overs when needed.

• Keep branding visual and audio cues consistent across versions.

### KEY BENEFITS FOR BRANDS LEVERAGING MULTILINGUAL SOCIAL PROOF

Producing testimonials in several languages helps your brand communicate directly with global customers, showing you truly understand and value their needs. This boosts trust, as viewers are more likely to believe and act upon stories delivered in their native tongue.

Additionally, multilingual videos often get more traction on social media, where users organically share content that reflects their cultures and experiences. Enhanced brand reputation follows, as you are seen as accessible, customer-focused, and receptive to multicultural perspectives.

International brands across sectors such as healthcare, finance, and technology have successfully harnessed this approach to expand market share. These companies report significant increases in engagement and conversion rates after adapting testimonial content for various regions.

Learning from these successes, you can adjust your own campaign to maximize the reach and impact of every testimonial. Focused investment in this area will pay dividends in both reputation and measurable marketing results. Overall, multilingual testimonial videos help brands bridge cultural gaps and drive loyalty worldwide.

## PROVEN TECHNIQUES FOR POWERFUL MULTILINGUAL TESTIMONIALS

Build a reliable process by testing translations and video segments with native speakers before launch. This extra step catches subtle language or cultural inaccuracies, ensuring maximum authenticity. Featuring genuine customers, rather than actors, elevates trust; remember that real stories always resonate best.

In addition, optimize your video’s metadata and captions to include relevant keywords in every supported language. Effective SEO strategies will help your testimonials rank higher in different countries, increasing organic reach and improving online visibility.

### SUMMARY

Multilingual testimonial production empowers your brand to connect authentically with global audiences. By investing in authentic, localized content, you reinforce customer trust and set your business apart. Each language version should reflect your message while honoring cultural distinctions and preferences. The result is greater brand recognition, higher conversions, and improved engagement in new markets. Embrace multilingual testimonials to ensure your voice resonates across languages and cultures.

Maintaining Consistent Brand Identity Across Digital Markets

# Maintaining Consistent Brand Identity Across Digital Markets

[March 24, 2025](https://growth-engines.com/insights/branding/maintaining-consistent-brand-identity-across-digital-markets)

Last Updated: March 4, 2026

![Digital Brand Consistency](https://growth-engines.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Digital-Brand-Consistency.jpg)

In today’s fragmented digital landscape, maintaining a consistent brand identity across multiple markets isn’t just good practice – it’s a strategic imperative. Companies expanding their digital presence globally face the significant challenge of preserving their core brand elements while adapting to diverse cultural contexts. This balance between consistency and localization determines whether your audience will recognize your brand regardless of where they encounter it.

## The Foundation of Digital Brand Consistency

A cohesive brand identity serves as the glue that holds your brand together across all digital touchpoints. When customers experience uniform brand presentation across all platforms, they develop stronger brand recognition and trust. According to recent industry research, brands with consistent presentation across all channels experience a 33% increase in revenue compared to those with inconsistent branding.

The foundation of brand consistency begins with establishing clear brand guidelines that document every aspect of your brand identity – from visual elements like logo usage and color palettes to your tone of voice and messaging frameworks. These guidelines become the reference point for all marketing efforts and ensure your brand maintains its core personality across various digital platforms. Implementing a comprehensive [global digital branding](https://growth-engines.com/insights/branding/global-digital-branding-strategies-for-expanding-your-online-presence-worldwide) approach is essential for organizations looking to maintain consistency at scale.

Digital markets present unique challenges for maintaining brand consistency that traditional markets don’t. The rapid pace of digital communication, the variety of formats required across different platforms, and the need to react quickly to market changes can all put pressure on consistent brand presentation. This is why implementing a strategic approach to [global digital strategy](https://growth-engines.com/insights/branding/developing-a-global-strategy-for-digital-branding-and-expanding-your-online-reach) becomes essential for success.

## Why Brand Consistency Matters in Digital Markets

The importance of brand consistency across all channels cannot be overstated in today’s digital environment. Here’s why it matters:

1. **Enhanced Brand Recognition**: When your digital presence across platforms shares consistent visual identity and messaging, customers are more likely to recognize your brand instantly. This recognition builds the familiarity that drives consumer preference.
2. **Increased Trust**: Consistency signals professionalism and reliability. When your brand elements remain consistent across all digital touchpoints, customers develop deeper trust in your offerings.
3. **Improved Customer Experience**: A consistent brand experience eliminates confusion and creates a seamless journey for customers as they interact with your brand across different platforms and regions.
4. **Greater Marketing Efficiency**: Well-defined guidelines streamline content creation and reduce the resources needed to maintain your brand across digital markets.
5. **Competitive Differentiation**: A strong, consistent brand identity helps differentiate your brand from competitors and establish a unique position in the market.

The benefits extend beyond marketing, consistent branding influences every aspect of customer perception and significantly impacts your ability to build [global digital recognition](https://growth-engines.com/insights/branding/global-digital-branding-strategies-for-expanding-your-online-presence-worldwide).

## Platforms That Centralize Brand Guidelines for Distributed Teams

As marketing teams scale globally and agencies, freelancers, and regional teams multiply, maintaining a single source of truth for brand guidelines becomes the defining challenge of brand management. Centralized brand guideline platforms solve this by creating a digital hub where every team member – anywhere, anytime – accesses current guidelines, downloads approved assets, and understands what is and is not permitted.

**Centralized brand platforms fall into three categories:**

### Digital Asset Management (DAM) tools

store, organize, and distribute brand assets with version control and permission management. They excel at asset libraries with thousands of files across multiple brands or markets.

### Brand portal platforms

host your complete brand identity system, color palettes, typography, tone of voice guidelines, logo variations, and usage examples, in an interactive, always-current format. Think of them as a living, web-based brand bible.

### Creative management platforms

combine brand guidelines, asset storage, workflow management, and approval processes into a single system, eliminating the email chain that typically governs brand approvals.

## Recommended tools by company size and need:

| Tool | Best For | Key Feature | Typical Price Range |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bynder | Enterprise, 500+ users, 100k+ assets | Advanced permissions, version control, custom workflows | $3,000–$15,000/month |
| Frontify | Mid-to-large teams (50–500 people) focused on brand systems | Interactive guidelines portal, component library, brand collaboration | $400–$2,000/month |
| Canto | Mid-market creative teams (50–300 people) | Smart search, metadata automation, fast onboarding | $1,500–$5,000/month |
| Canva for Teams | SMBs (5–50 people), design-heavy teams | Brand kit with locked colors/fonts, drag-and-drop templates | $180/year per brand |
| Brandfolder | Distributed field/sales teams, high-volume asset distribution | Real-time updates, download tracking, external sharing | $1,000–$4,000/month |

## Implementation tip:

Adoption is the real challenge, not technology. Set a ‘brand portal go-live date’ with a hard deadline after which the old Google Drive folder or Dropbox link will no longer be updated. This creates urgency that makes the transition stick within 2–3 weeks. Most teams reach 70–80% active usage within 30 days when migration is structured with a deadline.

## Maintaining Brand Consistency Across Cultures: The Global Localization Framework

The central tension in global brand management is this: consistency builds recognition, but relevance drives connection. The brands that win globally are those that know which elements to lock down universally and which to adapt by culture.

### Brand elements that must stay consistent globally:

Your logo (never stylized differently per market), core brand values (what your organization fundamentally stands for), quality standards (the level of execution buyers expect from your products or service), and brand voice fundamentals (whether your brand is formal or casual, authoritative or approachable).

### Brand elements that should adapt by culture:

Color meaning – white signals purity in Western markets but mourning in some East Asian cultures; red signals danger in some contexts and luck in others. Imagery – family structures, aspirational lifestyles, and work environments vary dramatically across cultures; using generic Western imagery in non-Western markets creates subtle disconnects that lower engagement. Tone formality – German audiences generally prefer precise, data-led messaging; Brazilian audiences respond to warmth and community. Seasonal emphasis – campaigns structured around Chri

# Multilingual e-learning video: 8 steps to success

Aug 24, 2023
/ Upd:
Jan 13, 2026


![Multilingual e-learning video: 8 steps to success](https://bluecarrot.io/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/blue-carrots-staff.png)

Senior Account Executive

## Introduction

In today’s interconnected world, where boundaries are increasingly blurred and diverse cultures intertwine, the demand for multilingual e-learning content is on the rise. As online education continues to gain popularity, the need to cater to a global audience becomes imperative. Developing multilingual e-learning video content is a strategic approach to engage learners from different linguistic backgrounds, ensuring inclusivity and maximizing educational impact.

Videos offer a dynamic and interactive learning experience that appeals to a wide range of learners. They can be a powerful teaching tool if done with all the effective guidelines ( _Kathryn E., “Effective Educational Videos: Principles and Guidelines for Maximizing Student Learning from Video Content”, CBE Life Sci Educ., 2016_) in mind. Educators can bridge the language gap by incorporating multiple languages into e-learning videos and reach learners worldwide, regardless of their native tongue. This not only enhances accessibility but also fosters a deeper understanding and engagement with the subject.

However, developing multilingual e-learning video content requires careful planning and execution. It goes beyond simply adding subtitles or dubbing existing videos. This article presents a comprehensive guide outlining eight essential steps to effectively develop multilingual e-learning video content. Whether you are an educator, instructional designer, or content creator, these steps will help you navigate the complexities of language diversity and create impactful e-learning experiences.

# How Video Translation Enhances Multilingual User Training for SSO and Access Management Systems

Cybersecurity

IAM

SSO

Localization

Enterprise Training

Tech


![Andrew Agarwal](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/b391942e71994fda3becc83d0d803730b74c65b839cb718f85013a739f850c52?s=96&d=mm&r=g)

Andrew Agarwal


Product Marketing Manager


[Read more blog of andrew-agarwal](https://ssojet.com/blog/author/andrew-agarwal)

November 26, 2025

5
min read


![How Video Translation Enhances Multilingual User Training for SSO and Access Management Systems](https://cdn.pseo.one/6853a4a8a2796a91bb994a76/687e6d61f6fe799d28851eff/topics/6926ef0f119d8bad8b8f8b3a/d0666e38-9914-4ccd-b40a-ada98594de5f.webp)

With the quick expansion of organisations across continents and oceans, there are now many new challenges. The onboarding and training of a workforce which is disbursed across the globe and has a multitude of various languages creates a level of complexity which, for Identity Access Management (IAM) systems, is especially great when that workforce is using IAM/SAA and SSO systems to gain access to corporate systems.

The traditional method of using text to deliver training and onboarding information to employees has been replaced by the new generation of employees. The current employees of most businesses today want to learn in ways that are more intuitive, accessible, and captivating than can be represented in a printed format.

In this blog, we take a closer look at how to leverage video translation to improve the effectiveness of multilingual training for companies that use SSO and access management systems.

## **The Complexity of Training Users on SSO and Access Management**

Systems that manage user access and Single Sign-On (SSO) are intended to simplify the authentication of users, but training for users can remain complicated, for a number of reasons:

### **1\. Multiple Login Flows and Interfaces**

Depending on the service, users might interact with:

- Browser plugins

- Mobile authenticator apps

- Conditional access prompts

- Multi-factor authentication (MFA) screens


### **2\. Security Sensitivity**

The inability to access critical systems may result in the locking of users out of systems and/or exposing potential security vulnerabilities. Properly training users can reduce the risk of this occurring.

### **3\. Rapid Feature Updates**

Due to changes in the login workflow and/or security policy of an organization, training content will continuously need to be updated in order to meet the latest requirements for user access.

### **4\. Global Workforce Diversity**

Many large organizations support their workforce across all areas of North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific and Latin America, with each geographic location having unique language needs.

## **Why Video Translation Matters for IAM and SSO Training?**

Video translation goes beyond simple subtitles. It includes:

- Translated on-screen text

- Voiceovers in target languages

- AI-generated dubbing for consistent narration

- Culturally adapted examples

- Localized UI screen recordings


### **1\. Makes Complex IAM Workflows Accessible to All Users**

Demonstrating the various technical issues of Identity and Access Management (IAM) systems is often much easier than explaining them verbally. Training Videos can make complicated demonstrations more visual but the user still needs to listen to the training video and follow the instructions.

[Video translation](https://murf.ai/tools/video-translator) ensures that:

- Global employees understand login flows exactly as intended

- Instructions are delivered in their native language

- Misinterpretation is minimized


### **2\. Reduces Security Risks Caused by Misunderstanding**

One of the biggest challenges in IAM is user error. Common mistakes include:

- Registering the wrong device for MFA

- Mismanaging recovery codes

- Failing to update authenticator apps

- Falling for phishing attempts due to confusion


### **3\. Improves Adoption of SSO Systems**

Adoption of IAM systems can be one of the most difficult parts of an IAM deployment. Change is often met with resistance, particularly when users must adapt to using new tools or following different security protocols.

Multilingual training accelerates adoption by:

- Making the system feel more familiar

- Reducing cognitive load on non-English speakers

- Allowing employees to follow step-by-step visuals and audio


### **4\. Ensures Consistency Across Regions and Departments**

Without video translation, regional IT teams are forced to:

- Create their own localized training material

- Verbally explain the process to users

- Interpret documentation on the fly


Centralized translated videos ensure:

- Uniform instructions

- Standardized onboarding

- Global alignment with corporate security policies


Organisations that operate in regulated industries, such as Financial Services, Healthcare, or Government, must ensure that all training materials comply with both Domestic and International laws.

### **5\. Supports Compliance and Audit Requirements**

In addition, many organisations are required to maintain proper audit trail documentation and have training documentation to demonstrate that they are following best practices.

Multilingual training videos serve as:

- Proof of standardized training delivery

- Evidence of compliance with global data security policies

- A foundation for user certification or login readiness programs


### **6\. Reduces IT Support Ticket Volume**

A common strain on IT helpdesks involves repetitive SSO-related tickets:

- “I can’t log in.”

- “My MFA code doesn’t work.”

- “I don’t understand this message.”

- “How do I reset my authenticator?”


The result is:

- Faster onboarding

- Lower support burden

- Less frustration for both users and IT departments


### **7\. Increases Engagement and Retention**

Research has proven that people learn better from video than they do from reading text; therefore, having translated and localized videos will increase engagement even more.

- Users feel more comfortable

- Training seems tailored to their needs

- Complex actions are demonstrated rather than explained


## **Best Practices for Translating SSO Training Videos**

To maximize impact, organizations should follow these best practices:

### **1\. Localize, Don’t Just Translate**

Terminology and examples used by IAM solutions must be adapted to each region/country to ensure users have the same understanding of the product within their respective regions.

### **2\. Use Screen Recordings in Local UI Languages**

The user interface of a video should be that of what they are going to encounter in the field.

### **3\. Offer Subtitles, Voiceovers, and Dubbing**

Each of the training videos caters to various accessibility needs and user preferences.

### **4\. Use AI Translation for Scalability**

When combined with Artificial Intelligence (AI), means organisations can provide an environment to create efficiencies in the translation of training video content, both in time and cost, creating better access.

### **5\. Keep Updates Frequent**

The IAM workflows of organizations change from time to time; therefore, videos must continually be updated.

## **Conclusion**

Video translations have changed from being optional to being critical for every company that has implemented or is using Single Sign-on (SSO) and Identity and Access management (IAM) systems throughout the world. By providing multilingual video content, businesses can enhance their ability to provide clear communication between different time zones; reduce potential exposure to cyber attacks via inverted security; provide an easier way for newly hired employees to get up to speed.

# How to Create a Content Localization Strategy for Customer Education

![How to Create a Content Localization Strategy for Customer Education](https://view.subpage.app/app/company/Cee1990b34d6f46aba51cdc2103a102c3/domain/M4ge1OMPUob/page/Mql6onaQUob/article/Mc9d3c56a910c1e1cd490412b4893c4df1734342237865/hero/Mbfff4aa57abb4b076e042dd3a86e8ce51734501803481.webp)

16 Dec 2024

Going global is a goal for many businesses, especially in today’s digital-first world. With SaaS products and services no longer confined to a single region, the potential for reaching a vast and diverse audience is greater than ever. However, expanding your reach is only the first step—truly connecting with your end users requires speaking their language, both literally and culturally.

Content localization bridges this gap, enabling businesses to deliver [customer education](https://wowto.ai/blog/A-Beginners-Guide-to-Customer-Education) in a way that resonates with users across different regions. Whether it’s through localized onboarding videos, product tutorials, or troubleshooting guides, tailoring content to meet the linguistic and cultural preferences of your audience ensures that your message is understood and valued.

In this blog, we’ll explore how to create an effective content localization strategy for customer education, helping you not only expand your global footprint but also foster stronger relationships with your customers around the world.

## **What is Content localization?**

Content localization is the process of adapting your content to meet the linguistic, cultural, and contextual preferences of a specific audience. Unlike basic translation, which focuses solely on converting text from one language to another, localization ensures that the content resonates deeply with the target audience. It involves more than just language; it incorporates local idioms, colloquialisms, and region-specific expressions that make the content feel natural and relatable.

Localization also addresses cultural nuances, such as symbols, colors, and imagery, which may have different meanings across regions. For example, while certain hand gestures or icons might be harmless in one culture, they could be offensive or misinterpreted in another. Additionally, it considers legal and regulatory differences, ensuring compliance with local standards and practices.

Beyond the surface, localization adapts the tone, style, and delivery of content to align with the communication norms of a specific market. Whether it's a formal tone for professional audiences or a casual, friendly approach for younger users, localization ensures that the content feels tailor-made for its intended audience. By making these adjustments, businesses not only bridge language gaps but also foster trust, improve user experience, and strengthen their connection with global customers.

## **Why Localization Matters**

Content localization is a strategic necessity for businesses aiming to succeed in the global marketplace. It allows companies to connect authentically with audiences, fostering engagement, loyalty, and growth. Just offering a universal product or service is no longer sufficient to engage customers from diverse regions. To build meaningful relationships, businesses must communicate in ways that resonate with local audiences. This goes beyond translation—it requires a deep understanding of cultural norms, local preferences, and specific customer needs. Content localization plays a significant role in creating these connections. Here's why it’s essential:

### **1\. Improves Customer Experience**

Customers expect personalized experiences, and localized content plays an important role in meeting these expectations. When users interact with materials—whether it's onboarding videos, support documentation, or product tutorials—that feel native to their culture and language, they’re more likely to have a positive experience. This personal touch enhances user satisfaction, fosters trust, and makes customers feel valued.

### **2\. Increases Engagement and Retention**

Engaging customers in their preferred language and cultural context leads to higher levels of interaction. Localized content not only captures attention but also holds it for longer. Whether it’s a step-by-step guide or a troubleshooting video, users are more likely to engage fully when the content feels relatable. This can significantly improve retention rates, as users are more inclined to return when they feel understood.

### **3\. Expands Market Reach**

Localization is a gateway to unlocking new markets. By tailoring content to specific regions, businesses can effectively reach audiences they might not have connected with otherwise. This allows for a broader market presence and opens the door to increased revenue opportunities. For instance, a SaaS company offering localized product guides in multiple languages can cater to diverse industries and regions, positioning itself as a truly global player.

### **4\. Aligns with Customer Success Goals**

A core objective of customer education is to empower users to succeed with your product or service. Localization ensures that all customers, regardless of their language or cultural background, have equal access to the knowledge they need. By providing clear, understandable, and culturally relevant content, companies can boost product adoption and reduce support queries, aligning closely with customer success initiatives.

### **5\. Strengthens Brand Reputation and Loyalty**

Brands that prioritize localization demonstrate a commitment to inclusivity and customer-centricity. This not only enhances their reputation but also fosters long-term loyalty. Customers are more likely to stick with a brand that goes the extra mile to meet their unique needs. Moreover, a strong localized presence signals to the market that your business is serious about serving diverse audiences and building a competitive edge.

### **6\. Drives Better ROI on Content**

Localized content often yields higher returns on investment. By tailoring content for specific audiences, businesses can increase their effectiveness, leading to better engagement metrics, higher conversion rates, and improved customer retention. A well-localized video tutorial or knowledge base article can drive significant value, ensuring the content works harder for the business across multiple regions.

## **Ways to Localize Customer Education Content**

Effectively localizing customer education content involves more than just translating text. It requires a comprehensive approach to ensure your materials resonate with your target audience on a linguistic, cultural, and contextual level. Here are key strategies to achieve successful localization:

### **1\. Translate Content Beyond Words**

Translation is the first step in content localization, but the true connection with a global audience goes deeper. It's not just about converting words from one language to another; it's about ensuring the meaning, tone, and intent remain intact. This involves understanding the subtle differences in how language is used across various regions.

For instance, consider the phrase "I’ll touch base with you later." While this idiom is common in American English, it might confuse non-native speakers or have no equivalent in other languages. A literal translation could lead to misunderstanding. Instead, localization ensures that the translated phrase conveys the same intent in a culturally familiar way, such as "I’ll follow up with you later" or an entirely different phrase that resonates with the target audience.

Additionally, regional variations of the same language often require attention. British English and American English differ in spelling (e.g., "color" vs. "color"), vocabulary ("lift" vs. "elevator"), and even grammar. Adapting content for specific regions ensures that it feels native to the audience, enhancing comprehension and en

## 5 ways of making more video out of your video

#### 1) Make video in several aspect ratios

You probably already know that different aspect ratios work better on different channels, for example:

• Youtube: 16x9

• LinkedIn: 1x1

• Stories: 9:16

So when you have made your video, publish it in different [video sizes for social media](https://storykit.io/blog/social-media-video-sizes).

#### 2) Customize the video for different platforms

This is not just about which aspect ratio you choose: you might want different tonality or images depending on which platform you publish on. Duplicate your video and make the changes, and voilà! Now you have even more versions.

#### 3) Customize the video for different target audiences

You probably have several target audiences, and they will surely be more attracted to your videos if you make some small adjustments. For example, maybe you want to adapt the imagery depending on the age the of your target groups? It’s simple to make several versions!

#### 4) Make the video for several markets

Do you have audiences in different markets and different languages? If so, it’s super easy to simply duplicate the video and get it translated! This is what the industrial giant SKF does for its 130 markets in Storykit.

# Creating a global content strategy to scale your business

June 13, 2023\|In [Articles](https://veracontent.com/category/articles/)\|By [Bridget Langer](https://veracontent.com/author/bridget-langer/)

![Creative multiethnic team of five skilled young colleagues working with data and marketing charts in modern office, leaning on the table.](https://veracontent.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Global-marketing-team-1024x683.jpg)

Content marketing generates three times as many leads as traditional outbound marketing and [costs 62% less](https://contentmarketinginstitute.com/articles/stats-invest-content-marketing/). It’s no secret that developing a solid global content marketing strategy is one of the best ways to scale a business.

High-quality content has the potential to build traction and drive long-term results. But ensuring this effectiveness expands across global markets can be challenging. Keep reading for more on what a global content strategy is and how to create an easily scalable one.

## **What is a global content strategy?**

![Marketing team discussing their global content strategy while looking at an iPad](https://veracontent.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Corporate-team-1024x683.jpg)

The need for content is universal. But if your brand is operating in different markets, you’ll need a global content strategy that caters to each audience’s specific language, context and culture.

**Global content strategies outline a framework for creating and managing content in multiple languages and regions**. They cover more than content planning but things like governance, user journeys, workflows and performance metrics.

It’s important that your global strategy not only focuses on creating content to highlight your brand and the problem you solve, but also how that content can be adapted to be relevant to global audiences.

Adapting and creating content for different markets is an incredibly detailed process that takes time and careful evaluation to get right. It’s well worth putting in the effort during the beginning stages of your global content strategy to make it easier to scale as you launch into different regions.

> _“I believe brands are built on content nowadays, especially with social media being so strong. You need content as a brand.” –_ [_Thiago Kiwi_](https://veracontent.com/mix/podcast/interview-thiago-kiwi-building-brand/) _, head of marketing & communications at_ [_Headspring Executive Development_](https://www.headspringexecutive.com/)

## **How do you create a scalable global content strategy?**

![The VeraContent management team, experts in creating global content strategies](https://veracontent.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/Vera-4-1024x768.jpg)

Setting up and maintaining a successful content strategy is much more manageable when you break it down into steps.

**Here are eight steps to follow when creating a scalable global content strategy:**

### **1\. Get clear on your goals and lay out a content roadmap**

As with any strategy, it’s essential to always start by outlining your goals. When doing so, include the people responsible for implementing the strategy. Even better, consult locals in each region. They’ll help you figure out the best route for achieving your goals.

At this point, you’ll also need to decide whether to take a [localized or standardized marketing](https://veracontent.com/mix/global-marketing-standardization/) approach. That means deciding whether your strategy will be standardized across all countries or if you’ll tailor it for each region. Each method has its pros and cons, but we usually recommend localizing your content marketing—at least in each of your key regions.

**Once your goals are clear, start planning.** Lay out a roadmap with timelines, milestones and responsibilities. If you’re starting with one new market at a time, plan when you want to add more regions into the mix—and what you want to achieve by each milestone.

> Remember, **a content roadmap isn’t set in stone**. You’ll likely veer off course at points, but it at least provides you with direction and goals to work towards.

### **2\. Set up a clear global content management structure**

Implementing a global content strategy involves many people—especially as more languages and regions are added. **That’s why it’s important to decide how best to manage your global content.**

For example, who will be responsible for creating, publishing and maintaining the content? Will you do so centrally, or will you opt for a decentralized model where each region maintains its content?

Another option is to maintain a centralized global content leadership team and outsource a [global content marketing agency](https://veracontent.com/) to create and manage content for each region.

### **3\. Select and properly set up a multilingual CMS**

A well-structured [multilingual content management system](https://veracontent.com/mix/multilingual-content-management-system/) (CMS) is _essential_ for implementing your global content strategy. Your CMS is where your content lives and if it’s not organized and set up correctly, it can become a nightmare to scale as your strategy expands.

Ideally, **you want a CMS with the capabilities to publish content in all required languages**. With a multilingual CMS, you can easily implement different language versions of your site and also provide a consistent customer experience across your website(s).

> **Popular multilingual CMSs include [WordPress](https://wordpress.org/), [Joomla](https://www.joomla.org/core-features.html), [Drupal](https://www.drupal.com/feature/multilingual), [Squarespace](https://www.squarespace.com/) and [Adobe Experience Manager](https://business.adobe.com/products/experience-manager/adobe-experience-manager.html?mv=affiliate&mv2=red).**

_See also:_ [_Multilingual content management: 5 tips to upload posts effectively_](https://veracontent.com/mix/multilingual-content-management/)

### **4\. Incorporate AI, automation and project management tools into your workflow**

> _“Right off the bat, automation offers time-saving.” – James Scherer, VP of growth sales at_ [_Codeless_](https://codeless.io/) _._

In order to scale your content marketing, you must incorporate some form of automation into your workflow. **Automation paired with AI and project management tools is the most efficient way to [create content at scale](https://veracontent.com/mix/podcast/interview-creating-content-scale/)**. It’s worth exploring various AI content tools to assist with tasks like creating content outlines, research, SEO, image creation and [machine translation](https://veracontent.com/mix/machine-translation-tools/).

Automation is a great way to ramp up your productivity by automating tasks like social media scheduling, email marketing and publishing blog posts. Project management tools help pull everyt
